Genre Masher 3000

AI-generated pitches that nobody asked for
Z-Image Ideogram 4 Krea
Most Wanted: The Reckoning Round
Weird West x Reality Competition

In the cursed boomtown of Gallows Gulch, the law has been replaced by a hit reality show, and grizzled bounty hunter Calloway "Deadeye" Pruitt is the reluctant frontrunner. To collect his largest bounty yet — a body-snatching outlaw who may also be a literal ghost — he must outlast eleven rivals on live television while a smiling host narrates his every blunder. Trouble is, Deadeye refuses to "play to camera," keeps shooting the confession-booth lantern, and the eliminated contestants don't go home — they get hanged. With the whole haunted town riding on the finale, he has one season to win, or the saloon gets foreclosed by demons.

Starring: Bounty Hunter
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Weird West and Reality Competition coexist uneasily, our hero — a Bounty Hunter — must save... something. Probably a town.

Mid-ground holds a jury-rigged wooden reality-show stage festooned with old film lights, a banner, and a glowing illuminated scoreboard of contestant portraits, several crossed out. Deep dust and volumetric light beams give cinematic depth.",

The Coroner's Constituent
Forensic Procedural x Cosmic Horror

State Senator Marian Voss has built her career on closing cases—and now, with the governorship within reach, she's personally consulting on a string of impossible autopsies to look tough on crime. But every corpse on the slab shares the same impossible cause of death, the same geometry carved into the bone, and the same name whispered from inside the body bag: hers. As her poll numbers climb and her sanity erodes, Marian must decide whether the truth is worth a thing no ballot can contain. Some platforms were not meant to be built on this Earth.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Forensic Procedural show had a baby with a Cosmic Horror miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Ambitious Politician.

High Noon at the Honeysuckle Inn
Cozy Whodunit x Revisionist Western

When dapper city detective Cassius "Cash" Delacroix rides into the dust-choked frontier town of Marrow's Bend to solve a tea-cozy poisoning, he finds eleven suspects, one shared bathhouse, and a sheriff who smolders like a struck match. Every interrogation drips with sweat and longing; every alibi smells of bergamot and gunpowder. As the body count rises faster than the saloon-keeper's pulse, Cash must crack the case before sundown — or before he himself is undone by the widow in mourning lace. Dread has never been this damp.

Starring: Private Investigator
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Cozy Whodunit x Revisionist Western. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Private Investigator.
Image prompt:

  "high_level_description": "A sweat-sheened frontier detective in a velvet waistcoat stands in a sun-scorched ghost town doorway, magnifying glass in one hand and revolver in the other, framed by a flickering lace curtain and a smoldering sheriff in the haze behind him.",
  "background": "A deserted high-plains main street at dusk, weathered clapboard buildings leaning in shadow with the gilded sign of the Honeysuckle Inn creaking overhead. Golden dust hangs thick in low raking light, with a distant figure silhouetted at the far end of the street. Heat shimmer blurs a porcelain teacup abandoned on a windowsill in the mid-ground.",

The Laudanum Ward
Revisionist Western x Psychological Horror

1887, a frontier mining town's only hospital, lit by gaslight and bad intentions. Night-shift nurse Corah Quint works the graveyard rotation alone, dispensing morphine to men who keep dying of the same wound — a wound that wasn't there yesterday. When a charming, unkillable gunslinger checks himself into Bed 9 and starts whispering things only her dead husband knew, Corah realizes the ward isn't full of patients. It's full of her. Slow-burn, sweat-soaked, and quietly terrifying, it's a fever dream where every recovery is a relapse.

Starring: Night-Shift Nurse
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Revisionist Western x Psychological Horror. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Night-Shift Nurse.
Image prompt:

  "high_level_description": "A lone night-shift nurse holds a guttering oil lamp at the foot of a frontier hospital bed where a smiling gunslinger lies, the dim ward stretching into darkness behind her.",

Faint gaslight bleeds from a far wall sconce, catching dust and laudanum vapor hanging in the air. Deep shadow swallows the back of the room, suggesting beds that may or may not be occupied.",

The Sampler: A Taste of Dread
Food Doc x Psychological Horror

Roy Tibble has sold premium artisanal salt to small towns for twenty-two years, and he's never met a customer he couldn't charm. But when his route lands him in Marender's Hollow — a fog-choked village obsessed with a "founder's stew" no one will describe — every doorbell he rings opens onto smiling neighbors who already know his name, his order, and his deepest fear. As the locals insist he taste "just one more spoonful," Roy realizes the documentary crew following him stopped blinking hours ago. A queasy, slow-burn dread about hospitality, hunger, and the unbearable politeness of people who want you for dinner.

Starring: Traveling Salesman
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Food Doc setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Psychological Horror. Our reluctant hero: a Traveling Salesman.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sweaty traveling salesman clutches his sample case on a fog-drowned village doorstep while an out-of-focus crowd of smiling locals holds out spoons toward him under sickly porch light.",
  "background": "A narrow cobblestone village lane at dusk, swallowed in thick low-lying fog that glows faint amber from gas lamps. Crooked timber cottages lean inward, their doorways spilling warm light, and a long communal dinner table stretches into the haze deep in the background. The depth fades into a milky vanishing point where indistinct figures stand unnaturally still.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, slow-burn psychological-horror grade, desaturated sickly greens and amber, photorealistic, heavy volumetric fog, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "dread, unsettling hospitality, queasy intimacy, folk-horror documentary realism, claustrophobic",
  "lighting": "low-key practical lighting from porch lamps casting long greasy shadows and rim-lighting the fog, with a cold blue fill creeping from off-frame.",

Thou Shalt Not Get Voted Off
Reality Competition x Surreal Comedy

Father Desmond Pellow lost his faith somewhere between the offering plate and the parish's leaking roof, so when a reality show called "Last Soul Standing" offers a $2 million prize, he signs up to compete against eleven strangers in a remote desert compound run by a smiling host who may or may not be God. The challenges are deeply unholy: assemble IKEA pews blindfolded, out-fast a competitive eater, deliver a sermon to a panel of judgmental flamingos. As the eliminations get weirder and the confessional booth starts talking back, Desmond must decide whether he's playing for the money, the redemption, or just the immunity idol shaped like a communion wafer. Faith is a game now. The tribe has spoken.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Reality Competition meets Surreal Comedy... but the protagonist is a Disillusioned Priest.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary priest in full vestments stands center-stage on a glowing reality-competition set in the middle of a desert, clutching a wafer-shaped immunity idol while stadium lights blaze behind him.",
  "background": "A vast cracked-earth desert at golden hour transformed into a slick reality-TV arena, with towering scaffold light rigs, a glowing host podium in the distance, and twelve numbered standing torches receding toward the horizon. Heat shimmer rises off the ground and a dramatic cloud bank glows orange. Camera cranes and boom mics are barely visible at the edges, blurred into the depth.",

  "lighting": "harsh stadium key light from above mixed with warm low sun, casting a long dramatic shadow and a faint halo glow behind the priest's head.",

Neon Dojo: The Accidental Fist
Cyberpunk x Martial Arts

Dao Pham is a sleep-deprived noodle-cart vendor in the perpetually raining megacity of Neo-Saigon-9 who just wants his loan repayments to stop auto-deducting. Then a dying monk uploads the legendary "Iron Lotus" combat protocol straight into Dao's bootleg cybernetic spine, and now every black-market warlord in the city wants his nervous system. Problem: the firmware only activates when Dao is genuinely annoyed, which is constantly, and it crashes every time he sneezes. With a snarky holographic mentor nagging in his ear, Dao must master a martial art he never asked for before the corporate ninjas reformat him into a toaster.

Starring: Reluctant Chosen One
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Reluctant Chosen One, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cyberpunk. The vibe? Unmistakably Martial Arts. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched young man in a hoodie stands in a rain-soaked neon alley, one fist glowing with electric-blue circuitry as holographic kung-fu glyphs spiral around him.",
  "background": "A narrow Neo-Saigon-9 back-alley at night, slick wet pavement mirroring towering walls of pink and cyan Vietnamese-Japanese neon signage receding into rainy fog. Steam vents and tangled cables hang overhead, and distant flying traffic streaks the deep-blue sky. Hard rain falls in visible sheets, catching every light source.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-and-magenta cyberpunk grade, photorealistic, heavy volumetric haze, wet-surface reflections, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "rain-soaked, neon-noir, gritty, electric, reluctant-hero stillness",
  "lighting": "moody low-key lighting with cyan and hot-pink neon rim light raking across the wet hero, a single blue glow blooming from his clenched fist.",

Chum: A Death at Sea Documentary
Sports Doc x Slasher

For forty years, Captain Augustus "Barnacle" Vane has guided his trawler crew to record-breaking marlin hauls — and a film crew is finally here to chronicle his legendary final season. But when deckhands start vanishing one foggy night and a hook-handed figure in a slicker begins picking off the team between interviews, the documentary becomes a fight for survival. Vane, who refuses to break character even while being chased, keeps narrating his own demise to the camera. The catch of the season may be the crew themselves.

Starring: Grizzled Sea Captain
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Sports Doc show had a baby with a Slasher miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Grizzled Sea Captain.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered sea captain stands at the helm of a fog-shrouded fishing trawler at night, lit by a documentary crew's harsh lamp, while a hooked killer's silhouette looms in the mist behind him on a blood-slicked deck.",
  "background": "A rusted commercial fishing trawler deck at midnight, slick with seawater and dark stains, ropes and gutting tables in the mid-ground. Thick rolling fog swallows the stern where a tall figure in a yellow rain slicker stands barely visible. Beyond the railing, a black churning ocean and a sliver of cold moon break through the cloud cover.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, handheld documentary framing, desaturated cold cinematic grade with sickly green highlights, photorealistic, heavy volumetric fog, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "gritty, dread-soaked, vérité realism, maritime menace, true-crime unease",
  "lighting": "a single harsh camera-mounted documentary lamp rakes across the captain's face from low-left while cold blue moonlight silhouettes the killer in the fog behind.",

The Last Draft: Pressure Front
Disaster x Cold Case

Reclusive novelist Mordecai Vane hasn't left his cliffside cabin in eleven years — not since the night his collaborator vanished and the case went cold. Now a record-breaking superstorm is tearing the coastline apart, and the floodwaters are unearthing evidence everyone assumed the ocean had swallowed forever. Trapped with a detective who never closed the file and a manuscript that reads suspiciously like a confession, Mordecai has until the seawall fails to figure out who really did it. Spoiler: it might be in chapter twelve.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Reclusive Author, alone, in the rain. The genre? Disaster. The vibe? Unmistakably Cold Case. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched, gray-bearded author in a soaked cardigan clutches a swollen manuscript on a collapsing cliffside deck as a monstrous storm surge explodes behind him, lit by lightning.",
  "background": "A storm-battered coastal cliff at night, a 1970s timber writing cabin half-torn open by wind, splintered shingles airborne. Behind it a towering wall of black ocean and rain rises against a bruised sky split by forked lightning. Deep wide-angle perspective with rain streaking the foreground and distant whitecaps fading into haze.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated steel-blue grade with warm lightning accents, photorealistic, volumetric rain and sea spray, practical lighting",
  "aesthetics": "ominous, somber, tense, weather-beaten, noir-meets-catastrophe",
  "lighting": "Cold blue ambient storm light raked by stark white lightning flashes, with a single warm lantern glow spilling from the broken cabin door.",
  "palette": [
    "#0E1A24",
    "#27414F",
    "#6E7C82",
    "#C9A86A",
    "#E8EDF0"
  ],

Hostile Takeover: Partners in Crime
Workplace Romance x Buddy Cop

Tech billionaire Dexter Vane has never filed his own expense report, let alone solved a corporate espionage case — so when his board assigns him a no-nonsense forensic auditor named Reyes to recover a stolen prototype, sparks fly in every wrong way. They argue over budgets, kick down server-room doors, and slow-dance at the company gala while wearing wires. He's a chaos-goblin who answers his own emails at 3 a.m.; she color-codes her ammunition. To save the company — and possibly each other — these mismatched partners must learn the deadliest skill of all: sharing a calendar.

Starring: Tech Billionaire
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Workplace Romance setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Buddy Cop. Our reluctant hero: a Tech Billionaire.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A glossy two-hander poster of a rumpled tech billionaire and a sharp forensic auditor standing back-to-back in a glass corporate atrium, half romance-poster glow and half action-thriller tension.",
  "background": "The soaring glass-and-steel lobby of a futuristic tech headquarters at dusk, floor-to-ceiling windows revealing a hazy city skyline below. Reflective marble floors throw long highlights, and a holographic company logo glows faintly behind them. Shafts of golden hour light cut through smoke and lens flare for cinematic depth.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-orange cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze, subtle lens flare",
  "aesthetics": "sleek corporate, flirtatious tension, action-glossy, big-budget two-hander",

Welcome to Mudgepenny: A Cosmic Inconvenience
First Contact x Cozy Whodunit

Constable Daphne Crick has been on the Mudgepenny village force for exactly nine days, and her biggest case so far was a stolen prize marrow. Then a 400-foot luminous starship parks itself in the duck pond and a beloved jam-judge turns up dead at the harvest fête. Now Daphne must solve a locked-greenhouse murder while interviewing seven gossiping pensioners and one polite, eight-eyed alien ambassador who insists it "comes in peace but does enjoy a good clue." With first contact and tea cooling fast, she has until the church bells ring to name the killer — terrestrial or otherwise.

Starring: Rookie Cop
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's First Contact meets Cozy Whodunit... but the protagonist is a Rookie Cop.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A nervous young rookie constable stands in a quaint English village square holding a notebook while an enormous glowing alien spaceship hovers silently over the duck pond behind her at golden dusk.",
  "background": "A picturesque cobblestone English village square with crooked timber-framed cottages, bunting strung between rooftops, and a small pond reflecting light. Looming above the thatched roofs is a vast, smooth, softly glowing saucer-shaped starship casting eerie blue light down onto the warm sunset scene. Wisps of evening mist and bunting flutter, with deep atmospheric perspective down the lane.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, warm golden-hour key with cool sci-fi rim light, photorealistic, fine film grain, volumetric haze, cinematic teal-and-amber grade",
  "aesthetics": "whimsical, eerie-cozy, gently absurd, charming, suspenseful",
  "lighting": "warm amber sunset glow on the village contrasted against the cold electric-blue underglow of the hovering spaceship.",
  "palette": [
    "#E9A24B",
    "#2C4A63",
    "#7FC8E8",
    "#F3E6C8",
    "#2A2620"
  ],
  "bg_brightness": 58,
  "elements": [
    {
      "type": "obj",
      "text": "",

The Ballad of Knuckles McGraw
Indie Folk Musical x Acid Western

Vincent "Knuckles" McGraw breaks kneecaps for the Genovetti family until a botched shakedown at a folk open-mic curses him to communicate only through plaintive acoustic ballads. To lift the hex, he must wander a sun-blasted, mescaline-warped frontier where every gunfight is settled by harmony and every clue is hidden in a murder ballad's third verse. Pursued by a banjo-wielding desert prophet and his own off-key conscience, Knuckles learns the only way out is to finger-pick the truth. It's a tender, hallucinatory showdown between a hitman and his feelings.

Starring: Mob Enforcer
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Mob Enforcer stumbles into a Indie Folk Musical conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Acid Western.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A scarred mob enforcer in a rumpled suit stands in a hallucinatory desert clutching an acoustic guitar like a shotgun, sun blazing behind him in surreal mirage colors.",
  "background": "A vast cracked-clay desert stretching to a warped horizon under a swollen blood-orange sun, heat shimmer distorting distant mesas into melting shapes. The sky bleeds from psychedelic violet to acid green at the edges, with a single weathered open-mic microphone stand planted in the sand far away. Dust devils curl through volumetric haze and the light feels feverish, like a fever dream at high noon.",

The Anointed Summer of Brayden Voss
Coming-of-Age x Conspiracy Doc

At seventeen, Brayden Voss thinks he's just charismatic — until a documentary crew arrives to film the backyard "wellness collective" he accidentally founded after a viral journaling post. Equal parts hormonal disaster and reluctant messiah, he must navigate first love, his learner's permit, and the seventeen adults now sleeping in his mom's garage who believe he can decode the moon. As the doc crew pushes for darker footage and a rival cult slides into his DMs, Brayden discovers that growing up and going full apocalypse-prophet feel disturbingly similar. Narrated entirely in breathy, ominous voiceover.

Starring: Cult Leader
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Coming-of-Age x Conspiracy Doc. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Cult Leader.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a backlit teenage cult leader standing barefoot in a sun-scorched suburban backyard, surrounded by kneeling adult followers, while a documentary camera looms in the foreground shadow.",
  "background": "A hazy late-afternoon suburban backyard with dry yellow grass, a sagging string of patio lights, and a vinyl-sided two-story house bathed in low golden sun. My Beast Friend
Creature Feature x Buddy Comedy

Agoraphobic horror novelist Marvin Pell hasn't left his cabin in eleven years — until a nine-foot lake creature crashes through his skylight, reads his unfinished manuscript, and declares it "honestly kind of mid." Now bound together by a curse neither understands, the prickly recluse and his unnervingly chatty amphibian roommate must survive a town of torch-wielding locals, a deadline-obsessed editor, and the horrifying ordeal of going outside. Marvin needs the monster's help to finish his book. The monster just needs a best friend. Somebody's getting eaten, and somebody's getting closure.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Reclusive Author, alone, in the rain. The genre? Creature Feature. The vibe? Unmistakably Buddy Comedy. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A grumpy, drenched author in pajamas shares an awkward porch swing with a towering scaly lake creature under stormy moonlight, both lit like a serious horror film but framed like a buddy comedy.",
  "background": "A remote wooden cabin porch at night in driving rain, deep pine forest receding into fog behind them. A misty lake glints faintly in the far background under a bruised, cloud-choked moon. Wet floorboards reflect cold light and warm porch-lamp glow, giving layered depth from foreground swing to distant treeline.",

The Gospel of the Drowning Stars
Mythic Retelling x Cosmic Horror

Father Elias Crane lost his faith somewhere between the third funeral and the second whiskey — until the saints in his cathedral's stained glass start weeping black seawater and whispering coordinates. Tasked with translating an "ancient miracle" beneath his flooded parish, Elias discovers the holy texts were never about God, but about Something older, wetter, and dreaming just offshore. Now the only lapsed priest in a fishing town must decide whether to perform the rites that wake it — or the heresy that might keep it sleeping. Faith was the easy part. Salvation has tentacles.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Disillusioned Priest, alone, in the rain. The genre? Mythic Retelling. The vibe? Unmistakably Cosmic Horror. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-soaked priest stands knee-deep in a flooded cathedral as a colossal eldritch silhouette coils in the storm-lit sky behind shattered stained glass.",
  "background": "The cracked interior of a half-submerged gothic cathedral, pews floating in black water, vaulted ceiling open to a churning bruise-colored storm. Through the broken rose window, a vast many-limbed shape rises from a distant ocean horizon, partly hidden in volumetric rain and lightning. Deep perspective draws the eye from the flooded nave to the impossible thing beyond the glass.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated teal-and-sickly-green cinematic grade, photorealistic, heavy volumetric haze, practical rain, film grain",

Mostly Magic: A Documentary
Mockumentary x Cozy Fantasy

Failed mural painter Dennis Crubbe agrees to let a film crew document his "comeback," which mostly involves crying into a microwave lasagna and overdrawing his rent. Then a tiny, judgmental hearth spirit named Pim moves into his radiator and starts critiquing his color choices — and slowly fixing his life with cottage magic, enchanted soup, and aggressively cozy houseplants. As the documentary lens drifts from "broke loser" to "reluctant fairy-tale wizard," Dennis must decide whether he's ready to be happy on camera. Warm, deadpan, and suspiciously emotional about a radiator gremlin.

Starring: Struggling Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Mockumentary in act one, Cozy Fantasy by act three, and a Struggling Artist sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a disheveled struggling artist in a cramped studio apartment holding a steaming microwave dinner while a glowing palm-sized hearth spirit hovers beside his radiator, documentary boom mic dipping into frame.",
  "background": "A cluttered low-rent studio apartment at golden hour, paint-splattered drop cloths, half-finished canvases stacked against peeling wallpaper, and a vintage cast-iron radiator. Soft warm light pools through a grimy window while string lights and overgrown houseplants creep magically across the shelves. Shallow depth blurs a documentary crew's silhouette and a tripod in the deep background.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, warm amber cinematic grade, photorealistic, fine film grain, naturalistic skin tones, soft volumetric window haze",
  "aesthetics": "cozy, deadpan, bittersweet, lived-in, whimsical realism, intimate",

Cold Case of the Heart
Paranormal Romance x Cold Case

Father Emil Vance lost his faith somewhere between funeral number four hundred and the discovery that his rectory's microwave is the only thing that loves him back. Then a heartbreakingly polite ghost named Lucinda starts haunting his confessional, asking him to solve her 1987 murder — and, awkwardly, to fall in love with her while he's at it. As Emil reopens the coldest case in the diocese, he must weigh decades-old evidence, an unholy crush, and the very real possibility he's just a sad man talking to a draft. Romance has never been this overdue, or this dead.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Paranormal Romance in act one, Cold Case by act three, and a Disillusioned Priest sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary priest stands in a candlelit church doorway as a translucent woman in 1980s clothing leans tenderly against his shoulder, a faded missing-person file clutched in his hand.",
  "background": "The cavernous interior of an aging Gothic stone church at night, with arched windows leaking pale blue moonlight and rows of flickering votive candles receding into deep shadow. A faint cold mist pools across the worn flagstone floor. Far in the background a cluttered evidence corkboard sits incongruously against a confessional booth.",

Grave Concerns: A Modern Embalming
Urban Fantasy x Satire

Mortimer Vane runs the last family-owned funeral home in a city where the dead won't shut up — literally, since the recently deceased now file complaints, demand five-star burials, and leave online reviews. When a venture-backed startup launches "AfterLyfe," a subscription service that resurrects the dead as compliant brand ambassadors, Mortimer becomes the only undertaker willing to let people actually stay buried. Armed with an enchanted embalming kit and a deeply unfashionable belief in dignity, he wages a polite, paperwork-heavy war against eternal life as a customer-loyalty program. Death, it turns out, has a marketing department.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Urban Fantasy show had a baby with a Satire miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Undertaker.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary undertaker in a black suit stands in a neon-lit funeral parlor where glowing translucent spirits queue at a reception desk like impatient customers, shot as a slick photoreal cinematic one-sheet.",

Ghostly blue-white spirits hover in an orderly queue receding into hazy depth, holding spectral numbered tickets. Stained-glass windows glow behind a sleek modern reception kiosk, blending Gothic gravity with sterile startup branding.",

The Vanishing Cabinet Protocol
Spy Thriller x Locked Room

Washed-up Vegas illusionist Cornelius "The Astonishing" Vale gets handed the worst gig of his career: a foreign intelligence service has sealed the only access panel to a nuclear failsafe inside a vault that locks from the inside, and the lone witness vanished mid-trick. With his sequined assistant doubling as a reluctant MI6 liaison and a deck of trick cards as his only weapon, Cornelius has 90 minutes to misdirect a room full of armed spies, solve who locked it, and pick a deadbolt with a dove. The catch: nobody left the room, and one of them isn't real. Prepare to be deceived — for your country.

Starring: Stage Magician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Spy Thriller and Locked Room coexist uneasily, our hero — a Stage Magician — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A tuxedoed stage magician stands inside a steel vault palming a glowing keycard like a playing card, spotlit dramatically as shadowy suited figures loom around the sealed door behind him.",
  "background": "The interior of a windowless reinforced bank vault rendered in cold steel and brushed chrome, a massive circular blast door sealed shut at the rear with red status lights blinking. Volumetric haze drifts through a single overhead theatrical spotlight, and faint cigarette smoke curls in the beam. Deep shadows pool in the corners where indistinct men in dark suits stand half-hidden.",

Honk If You Believe: The Glorbax Testimonies
Alien Invasion x Mockumentary

A documentary crew follows Brother Dale Pucket, charismatic leader of the Sedona-based Order of Eternal Upward Mobility, as he insists the mothership hovering over his compound is here specifically to collect him and his $400 starter robes. When the actual Glorbax invasion fleet begins vaporizing the suburbs, Dale reframes it as a "membership drive" and frantically sells parking spots in the apocalypse. The aliens, baffled, request a meeting with management. It's a faith-based feature about commitment, branding, and not reading the fine print on first contact.

Starring: Cult Leader
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Alien Invasion meets Mockumentary... but the protagonist is a Cult Leader.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a beaming cult leader in white robes gesturing toward a colossal alien mothership while a documentary boom mic dips into frame, shot like a real comedy mockumentary still.",

A vast metallic alien mothership hangs low and impossibly close in the dusty sky, casting a hard shadow over the gathering. Lens flares and floating dust hint at handheld documentary realism.",

Code Blue: The Last Shift
Post-Apocalyptic x Slasher

Twelve years after the bombs fell, Marlene Voss runs the night shift at the last functioning hospital on Earth — a flickering wing powered by a bicycle generator and sheer spite. When a masked figure in a gas mask and surgical scrubs starts "discharging" survivors one by one, Marlene must protect her six remaining patients with nothing but a defibrillator, a clipboard, and a strict no-running-in-the-hallways policy. She hasn't slept since the apocalypse and she is NOT about to start a paperwork backlog now. The Reaper keeps regular hours; so does she.

Starring: Night-Shift Nurse
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Post-Apocalyptic meets Slasher... but the protagonist is a Night-Shift Nurse.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary night-shift nurse stands defiant in a ruined hospital corridor, raising glowing defibrillator paddles as a gas-masked killer looms in the smoke behind her.",
  "background": "A collapsing hospital corridor lit by failing emergency strips, paint peeling and ceiling tiles hanging, gurneys overturned in the haze. Through a shattered window at the far end glows a dead, ash-orange post-apocalyptic skyline with toppled towers. Volumetric smoke drifts under flickering fluorescents, deep perspective receding into darkness.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated teal-and-sodium grade, heavy film grain, photorealistic, volumetric haze, practical flickering light",
  "aesthetics": "grimy, claustrophobic, dread-soaked, gritty survival horror, cinematic tension",
  "lighting": "harsh stuttering fluorescent overheads cut by an electric-blue glow from the defibrillator paddles, the killer half-swallowed in shadow.",

  ]

The Grimm Ward: A Surgeon's Cure
Fairy Tale x Body Horror

Dr. Margit Vasco hasn't slept in nine years and has stopped believing fairy tales can hurt you — until a coughing child in her ER hacks up a living, blinking rosebud. Soon her patients are sprouting briar-thorn ribs, weeping golden teeth, and growing extra thumbs that whistle lullabies. Margit, who medicates exhaustion with vending-machine coffee and dark sarcasm, realizes the curse is contagious and she's Patient Zero of a "happily ever after" rewriting her own anatomy. To break the spell she must operate on herself before the storybook finishes the ending. Some cures are worse than the fairy tale.

Starring: Burned-Out Surgeon
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Fairy Tale show had a baby with a Body Horror miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Burned-Out Surgeon.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A haggard surgeon stands in a fog-choked operating theater, her surgical gown blooming with living thorny roses that erupt from her own forearm, lit like a prestige horror one-sheet.",
  "background": "A derelict operating theater at night, tiled walls cracked and weeping condensation, an overhead surgical lamp casting a cold cone of light through drifting volumetric haze. Behind her, gurneys recede into darkness where faint storybook-gold light glows from a half-open ward door. Depth falls off into deep shadow with a sickly green emergency exit glow at the far end.",

Sunset Boulevard of the Damned
Gothic Horror x Jukebox Musical

Faded screen siren Vesper Lamont hasn't left her crumbling Hollywood mansion in forty years — and neither have the studio executives she buried in the rose garden. When a young location scout reawakens the house, Vesper discovers her glory days return whenever she sings, draining decades off everyone who joins the chorus. Now she's staging the deadliest comeback in history: a full-throated jukebox spectacular powered by the souls of her audience. The encore is eternal. The reviews are murderous.

Starring: Aging Movie Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Gothic Horror x Jukebox Musical. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Aging Movie Star.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A glamorous aging actress in a moth-eaten silver gown stands mid-song on a decaying grand staircase, candle-lit and surrounded by drifting dust and pale spectral admirers, shot like a prestige gothic-horror film still.",
  "background": "The interior of a vast, rotting 1920s Hollywood mansion ballroom with a sweeping marble staircase choked in cobwebs and dead ivy. Tall arched windows leak silver moonlight through grime, and a shattered crystal chandelier hangs at a sinister tilt overhead. Deep shadows pool in the corners while volumetric haze drifts across the frame, giving enormous theatrical depth.",

Desert Storm: A Ballad of Lies
Military Action x Tragic Musical

Corporal "Smiling" Vance Mireles never enlisted — he forged the papers, stole the rank, and conned an entire forward operating base into believing he's a decorated war hero. The trouble is, the only way he can rally his doomed platoon out of an ambush is by singing — and every soaring power-ballad he belts reveals one more devastating lie about who he really is. As the artillery falls in slow-motion and the strings swell, Vance must choose between the magnificent fraud that made him beloved and the truth that could save the men who'd die for him. It's a heartbreaking, brass-and-tears musical about a man whose finest performance is also his last.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Military Action meets Tragic Musical... but the protagonist is a Con Artist.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A battle-worn con-man soldier stands singing mid-firefight in a smoke-choked desert, arms outstretched as tracer fire streaks behind him like stage lights, tears cutting through the grime on his face.",
  "background": "A sprawling war-torn desert valley at dusk, distant burning vehicles and rising columns of black smoke against a bruised violet sky. Tracer rounds and falling flares streak diagonally like spotlights, catching swirling dust and sand. Deep haze gives layered atmospheric depth, with shadowy soldiers crouched far behind the central figure.",

The Ghostwriter's Last Inning
Cold Case x Sports Doc

Reclusive novelist Sol Ardway hasn't left his rain-soaked cabin in nineteen years — not since the night his ghostwritten sports memoir went to print and its subject, a beloved minor-league pitcher, vanished without a trace. Now a fame-hungry documentary crew has talked their way onto his porch, convinced the unsolved disappearance is buried somewhere in Sol's unpublished final chapter. As cold-case clues collide with slow-motion replays and confessional talking-heads, Sol must decide whether the perfect ending he invented was also the perfect alibi. Some stories were never meant to be edited.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Reclusive Author, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cold Case. The vibe? Unmistakably Sports Doc. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched, gray-bearded reclusive author stands on a creaking porch clutching a manuscript, lit by a documentary crew's harsh lamp while an old baseball glove rests on the railing.",
  "background": "A remote weathered timber cabin at dusk, half-swallowed by pine forest and sheeting rain. Behind the porch a faint floodlit baseball diamond glows ghostly through the mist, its backstop and outfield fence barely visible. Deep atmospheric haze separates the foreground porch from the distant abandoned ballfield, giving cold, lonely depth.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated steel-blue cinematic grade, photorealistic, heavy volumetric rain and haze, naturalistic documentary-style framing",

High Noon at Heat Death
Spaghetti Western x Hard Sci-Fi

Newly badged Deputy Wren Calloway patrols Perdition Flats, a dust-choked frontier town orbiting a dying red dwarf, where every duel is settled not by who draws fastest but by who correctly calculates relativistic bullet drift first. When a notorious outlaw arrives claiming he can violate the second law of thermodynamics, Wren — who faints at the sight of entropy — must master orbital mechanics, peer review, and the quick-draw before sundown takes 11.7 minutes to arrive. The townsfolk debate it in a saloon that doubles as a cryogenics lab. It's the slowest fastest gun in the galaxy.

Starring: Rookie Cop
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Spaghetti Western setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Hard Sci-Fi. Our reluctant hero: a Rookie Cop.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A young deputy in a dust-caked duster stands in a cracked desert street beneath two suns and a vast ringed gas giant on the horizon, hand hovering over a holstered brass-and-copper rail-revolver at high noon.",
  "background": "A wide spaghetti-western frontier street of weathered clapboard buildings baked in ochre dust, receding to a flat cracked-earth horizon. Above hangs an enormous banded gas giant with thin glinting rings, flanked by a small fierce red dwarf sun, casting layered crimson and amber light. Heat shimmer and fine drifting dust hazes the deep background; faint solar-collector windmills stand where saloon signs would be.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, wide-angle hero framing, dusty teal-and-amber cinematic grade, lens flare from twin suns, photorealistic, volumetric dust haze, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "sun-scorched, austere, tense, lonesome, hard-edged sci-fi gritty western",
  "lighting": "harsh dual-source noon light — warm red-dwarf glow plus a cold secondary fill — throwing long crisp double shadows across the cracked street.",

Probable Cause: The Vault of Sweetwater
Heist x Legal Drama

Sheriff Delilah "Del" Caruthers runs the laziest county in Texas — until the slickest crew in the Southwest rolls into Sweetwater to crack the First Federal vault, and their ringleader turns out to be the defense attorney she once kissed at law school and never got over. When the heist goes wrong and bodies need explaining, Del has to prosecute the case AND chase the loot, all while pretending her courtroom voice doesn't go husky every time she objects. It's slow-burn dread with sweat on the upper lip. Justice has never been this damp.

Starring: Small-Town Sheriff
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Heist x Legal Drama. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Small-Town Sheriff.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a small-town sheriff in a tense courthouse hallway, badge gleaming, with a stolen-cash vault and a charming defense attorney looming behind her in sultry shadow.",

Deep behind her, the open steel door of a bank vault glows faintly, stacks of disturbed cash visible in the gloom. Dust motes hang in the heavy heat-shimmered air, giving the whole space a humid, breathless depth.",

The Last Card: A Prophecy of Larceny
High Fantasy x Heist

Madame Vesryn, a velvet-draped seer who can read any future except her own, owes a blood-debt to the Archmage of Thornhold — and his vault holds the one relic that could rewrite her fate. So she assembles a crew: a sweat-slick lockpicking paladin, an enchantress who flirts as fluently as she lies, and a goblin who keeps proposing. The catch: every tarot pull warns that one of them dies tonight, and Vesryn refuses to ask which. Equal parts doom, longing, and grand-larceny, it's a candlelit con where destiny is just another mark to be robbed.

Starring: Fortune Teller
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: High Fantasy x Heist. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Fortune Teller.
Image prompt:

  "high_level_description": "A sultry hooded fortune teller spreads glowing tarot cards before a colossal enchanted vault, her heist crew silhouetted in candlelit shadow behind her, rendered as a moody photoreal fantasy film still.",

Drifting motes of magic and dust hang in the smoky air.",

Cold Cuts: A Case Gone Cold
Cold Case x Food Doc

Dr. Vance Marlow hasn't held a scalpel since the surgery that ended his career — now he holds a tasting spoon. Lured out of retirement to crack a 22-year-old unsolved murder, he treats the frozen evidence like a Michelin tasting menu, lovingly narrating each blood-spatter pattern and decomposing clue in hushed, reverent foodie tones. As suspects "plate" their alibis and a rival detective sears the truth tableside, Vance discovers the killer left a signature as distinctive as a reduction. Equal parts forensic thriller and prestige cooking show, justice has never looked so deliciously overplated.

Starring: Burned-Out Surgeon
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Cold Case setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Food Doc. Our reluctant hero: a Burned-Out Surgeon.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary surgeon-turned-detective lit like a celebrity chef, leaning over a stainless-steel autopsy table arranged like a gourmet tasting plate, in a cold morgue bathed in moody documentary light.",
  "background": "A dim forensic morgue with brushed stainless-steel walls and a wall of frosted refrigerated drawers receding into shallow-focus darkness. Cold blue overhead panels glow faintly while a single warm key light spills across the central exam table. Wisps of refrigerated vapor drift low across the polished floor, giving depth and chill.",

Dead Man's Reverb
Hardboiled Detective x Rock Opera

Detective Frankie "Knuckles" Malone got bounced off the force for tampering with evidence — and also for sobbing during interrogations. Now a washed-up gumshoe haunting a rain-soaked city, he stumbles onto a murder that can only be solved by feeling things, loudly, in four-part harmony. As the case cracks open, so does Frankie: the noir voiceover gives way to power ballads, the precinct becomes a strobe-lit arena, and his microwave lasagna becomes a recurring duet partner. To catch the killer, he must do the one thing a hardboiled man fears most — hit the high note.

Starring: Disgraced Detective
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Hardboiled Detective in act one, Rock Opera by act three, and a Disgraced Detective sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-drenched detective in a trench coat clutches a chrome microphone like a service revolver under a single noir streetlight, while electric stage light bleeds in from above.",
  "background": "A narrow, wet alley in a 1970s metropolis at 3 a.m., brick walls slick with rain and neon runoff, a flickering bar sign deep in the haze. The foreground is gritty noir darkness; the upper third explodes into the violet-and-magenta glow of an unseen rock arena spilling over the rooftops. Steam rises from a manhole and catches the colored light, blending detective gloom with concert spectacle.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, wet-street reflections, teal-and-magenta cinematic grade, volumetric stage haze, photorealistic, fine film grain",

The Marks of Blackthorn Manor
Psychological Drama x Gothic Horror

Slick grifter Ezra Voss has scammed his way into a cushy "inheritance" at a fog-drowned country estate, certain the grieving widow is his easiest mark yet. But the candles relight themselves, the portraits keep score, and the house seems to know every lie he's ever told. As Ezra's confidence games unravel into something far more intimate — and the manor begins billing him interest on his sins — he realizes the only con left is convincing a haunted house he's worth keeping alive. To survive the night, the world's best liar must do the one thing he never has: mean it.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Con Artist discovers their quiet life is actually a Psychological Drama, they must master the genre conventions of Gothic Horror to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sharply dressed con man in a candlelit gothic manor stares back at his own reflection in a cracked antique mirror as shadowy hands emerge from the dark behind him, shot like a prestige psychological horror one-sheet.",
  "background": "The grand decaying foyer of a Victorian manor at midnight, peeling damask wallpaper and a sweeping staircase swallowed by darkness. Thick cold fog pools across a checkerboard marble floor lit only by guttering candelabra. Deep shadows climb the walls and the far end of the hall dissolves into impenetrable black, giving immense vertical depth.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, low-key chiaroscuro lighting, desaturated cold grade with warm candle accents, fine film grain, volumetric haze, photorealistic",
  "aesthetics": "gothic, ominous, opulent decay, psychological dread, prestige horror",
  "lighting": "warm flickering candlelight rims the figure from one side while everything else falls into deep blue-black shadow, with a faint cold moonlight wash from above.",

Till Cover Do Us Part
Marriage of Convenience x Spy Thriller

Senator-hopeful Diane Plimpton-Vasquez needs a spotless family image; freelance asset Marcus "Three Passports" Vane needs a legal address that isn't a safehouse. Their fake marriage is a flawless poll-booster — until her opponent turns out to run an arms cartel and Marcus's "consulting job" turns out to be assassinating it. Now Diane must survive a gala shootout, a leaked sex tape that's actually a dead-drop, and the crushing realization that the only man who's ever remembered her birthday does it because it's also a detonation code. Between debates she cries into a microwave lasagna. He finds it oddly endearing.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Marriage of Convenience in act one, Spy Thriller by act three, and a Ambitious Politician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A glamorous power couple in formalwear stand back-to-back on a campaign stage, she clutching a podium microphone, he palming a silenced pistol behind a bouquet, confetti and muzzle flash mingling in the air.",
  "background": "A grand convention hall draped in patriotic bunting and balloon arches, deep rows of empty seats receding into blue stage haze. Overhead spotlights cut hard cones through drifting confetti. Far upstage a giant campaign banner glows out of focus, its slogan unreadable behind volumetric smoke.",

Sleight of Hand: The Disappearing Asset
True Crime x Spy Thriller

Mild-mannered illusionist Roland "The Astonishing" Beck thinks his biggest secret is how he palms a half-dollar. Then a leaked surveillance tape reveals his stage assistant has been laundering state secrets through the vanishing-dove act for a decade — and Roland's name is on every dead drop. With a reluctant documentary crew filming his every move and three intelligence agencies certain he's the most dangerous double agent alive, Roland must weaponize misdirection, escape artistry, and one extremely real rabbit to clear his name. Turns out "now you see me" is excellent tradecraft.

Starring: Stage Magician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Stage Magician discovers their quiet life is actually a True Crime, they must master the genre conventions of Spy Thriller to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A tuxedoed stage magician strides through a rain-slick European alley clutching a smoking handcuff while red laser tripwires and a surveillance-photo grid frame him like an intelligence target.",
  "background": "A narrow nighttime European cobblestone alley slick with rain, neon signage bleeding red and amber into puddles, distant theater marquee glowing out of focus. Crimson laser tripwires crisscross the mid-depth, and a faint grid of pinned black-and-white surveillance photographs overlays the upper corners like an evidence board. Atmospheric haze and shallow depth give the scene cinematic tension.",

The Whistleblower's Skin
Body Horror x Noir

Mid-level compliance analyst Dale Voss thought he was just reporting expense fraud — until he found the memo that explains why everyone in Accounts Payable is growing a second mouth. Now the only person who believes him is the chain-smoking ghost of his own conscience, and the only way out is to follow the oldest rule in the book: trust no one, especially the guy in the mirror who keeps shedding. As Synthrex Corporation "onboards" its employees into something wetter, Dale chases a paper trail that bleeds. The truth is in the building. So is whatever the building is becoming.

Starring: Corporate Whistleblower
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Corporate Whistleblower stumbles into a Body Horror conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Noir.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-slicked noir office corridor where a sweating man in a soaked trench coat clutches a folder, his shadow on the wall splitting open into a writhing fleshy seam.",
  "background": "A long, dim corporate hallway at night, fluorescent ceiling panels flickering and reflecting in wet linoleum that looks impossibly rain-slicked indoors. Venetian-blind shadows slice across the walls from an unseen window, and deep in the receding perspective a single open doorway glows a sickly biological pink. Faint condensation and steam hang in the cold air, suggesting the building itself is breathing.",

The Skip-Trace of Mawr County
First Contact x Cosmic Horror

Disgraced bounty hunter Cassidy "Coyote" Reyes tracks fugitives for a living, until her latest bounty turns out to be the only human who knows that the friendly aliens who landed in Mawr County, Nebraska, are merely the larval stage of something with too many angles to look at directly. Now her quarry is the one warning her to run. As the townsfolk start humming the same impossible note in their sleep, Coyote must decide whether to collect her paycheck or save the seven thousand people who keep insisting everything is fine. Her quirks: she narrates her own arrests, and she cannot stop sketching the geometry that is slowly unmaking her mind.

Starring: Bounty Hunter
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where First Contact and Cosmic Horror coexist uneasily, our hero — a Bounty Hunter — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A lone bounty hunter stands in a Nebraska cornfield at dusk, silhouetted against a vast non-Euclidean alien structure tearing open the sky, photographed like a prestige sci-fi horror one-sheet.",
  "background": "A flat endless cornfield under a bruised twilight sky, rows of corn receding to a low horizon. Above the field, an impossible alien geometry — interlocking rings and angles that hurt to follow — hangs partially open like a wound, leaking pale luminous mist down over a tiny grain-silo town in the distance. Volumetric haze and drifting particulate fill the deep middle ground.",

The Bloodhound Protocol
Gothic Horror x Spy Thriller

Disgraced intelligence-agency bounty hunter Vesper Crane tracks her targets by scent and a "small" condition she'd rather not file in her HR paperwork: she's been dead since Tuesday. Tasked with extracting a defecting count from a fog-drowned Carpathian embassy, she discovers the mark is also her ex-handler, also a vampire, and also extremely still in love with her. As assassins in tuxedos repel down crumbling cathedral walls, Vesper must decide whether to collect the bounty or the kiss. Dread, double agents, and one very ill-advised candlelit waltz ensue.

Starring: Bounty Hunter
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Gothic Horror x Spy Thriller. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Bounty Hunter.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A pale, glamorous female bounty hunter in a wet trench coat stands before a fog-shrouded gothic cathedral, silenced pistol lowered, fangs barely visible, blood-red moon behind her",
  "background": "A towering ruined gothic cathedral at midnight, flying buttresses and broken stained-glass windows looming through thick rolling fog. A swollen blood-red moon hangs low between the spires, backlighting drifting mist. Deep stone canyon of the embassy courtyard recedes into darkness with faint amber window glows.",

Little League of the Damned
Sports Doc x Found Footage

Divorced dad Greg Pernicki just wanted to film his daughter's championship Little League season for the grandparents. But somewhere between the third inning and the concession stand, the camcorder starts catching things no scoreboard can explain — a base coach who never blinks, dugout chants in a language nobody taught the kids, and an umpire who calls strikes before the pitch is thrown. As parents vanish one tailgate at a time, Greg keeps rolling, because the only thing scarier than the thing in left field is missing Lily's first home run. Shot entirely on one increasingly cursed handheld camera.

Starring: Single Parent
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Sports Doc setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Found Footage. Our reluctant hero: a Single Parent.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal found-footage horror poster framed inside a camcorder viewfinder, where a terrified dad films an empty floodlit Little League field at night with one tiny figure standing motionless on the pitcher's mound.",
  "background": "A small-town Little League baseball diamond at night under buzzing sodium floodlights, chain-link backstop in the foreground and a dark treeline swallowing the outfield. Heavy fog rolls across the infield dirt and an empty aluminum bleacher sits abandoned to the side. The whole image is wrapped in a slightly distorted camcorder-screen vignette with REC indicators and timecode burn-in.",
  "art_style": "shot on a consumer camcorder aesthetic, photorealistic, handheld motion blur, low-light digital noise, blown-out floodlight highlights, desaturated cold grade with sickly green cast, lens vignette, interlacing artifacts",
  "aesthetics": "found-footage dread, suburban americana decay, uncanny stillness, grainy authenticity",
  "lighting": "harsh overhead stadium floodlights cutting through fog with deep black shadows and a faint glow from the camera screen on the operator's face.",
  "palette": [
    "#0c1410",
    "#3f5a3a",
    "#9aa86c",
    "#d8d2b0",
    "#1b1b1b"
  ],

  "elements": [

      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.36,
      "y": 0.4,
      "w": 0.28,
      "h": 0.34
    },
    {

Code Blue: The Comeback Routine
Dance Musical x Sports Doc

Marisol Vega works the 3 a.m. shift on the cardiac floor, where she's secretly choreographed an entire jazz-funk number around the rhythm of the heart monitors. When a rival hospital challenges her ward to the National Defibrillator Dance-Off, a deadpan documentary crew embeds with the team to capture every grueling rehearsal, every sequined scrub, every blistered clog. Part triumphant musical, part sweat-soaked sports doc, it follows one exhausted nurse chasing glory between vitals checks. Will she flatline — or bring the house down before sunrise?

Starring: Night-Shift Nurse
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Night-Shift Nurse, alone, in the rain. The genre? Dance Musical. The vibe? Unmistakably Sports Doc. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched night-shift nurse in teal scrubs strikes a triumphant dance pose under hospital floodlights in a rain-slicked ambulance bay, shot like a gritty sports documentary one-sheet.",
  "background": "A rain-soaked hospital ambulance bay at 3 a.m., wet asphalt mirroring red EMERGENCY signage and harsh sodium floodlights. Deep behind, blurred sliding glass doors glow clinical white and an idle ambulance sits in soft focus. Cold mist drifts through the beams while puddles ripple with falling rain.",

Last One Standing: The Lighthouse Gauntlet
Reality Competition x Martial Arts

Bartholomew "Barty" Quill has tended the Stormgrave Lighthouse alone for nineteen peaceful years — until a foghorn blares, stadium lights ignite the cliffs, and a host announces he's been the unwitting star of "Last One Standing," the world's deadliest reality competition. Now ninety-three eliminated contestants want his immunity lantern, and the only way off the island is to win. Armed with a mop, a thermos of chowder, and martial arts he absorbed from late-night VHS tapes, mild-mannered Barty must roundhouse his way to a finale. The cameras never blink. Neither can he.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Lighthouse Keeper discovers their quiet life is actually a Reality Competition, they must master the genre conventions of Martial Arts to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered middle-aged lighthouse keeper strikes a martial-arts stance atop a storm-lashed cliff while caged stadium lights and floating reality-TV cameras surround him, photographed like a gritty action one-sheet.",
  "background": "A jagged rocky island at dusk crowned by a towering candy-striped lighthouse, its beam slicing through rolling sea fog. Crashing waves explode against the cliffs in the midground while distant floodlight rigs and camera drones hover in the storm clouds, betraying the secret TV set. Deep atmospheric haze gives layered depth from foreground rocks to the glowing horizon.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-orange cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze, fine film grain, dramatic rim light",
  "aesthetics": "gritty, epic, absurd-deadpan, rain-soaked, high-stakes spectacle",

Crystal Ball and Chain
Indie Folk Musical x Buddy Cop

Detective Roma Velasquez can see exactly how everyone dies — except her insufferably handsome new partner, Detective Cole Briggs, whose future is a humming blank she finds infuriatingly, dangerously attractive. Forced to solve a string of murders she keeps predicting one verse too late, the pair process every clue, hunch, and unbearable longing in fingerpicked acoustic ballads. As a hooded killer closes in, Roma must decide whether to trust her tarot or the man whose death she cannot foresee. It's tender. It's sweaty. It's harmonizing about probable cause.

Starring: Fortune Teller
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Indie Folk Musical x Buddy Cop. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Fortune Teller.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal two-shot of a soulful female fortune-teller cop and her brooding partner pressed back-to-back over a glowing crystal ball on a neon-soaked rain-slicked street, framed like a moody buddy-cop one-sheet.",
  "background": "A rain-drenched night alley in a small port city, brick walls plastered with peeling band flyers and tarot posters, wet asphalt mirroring violet and amber neon. Deep shallow-focus haze pushes a distant police cruiser's blue strobe into soft bokeh. A single hanging fairy-light string sags between fire escapes, glowing warm against the cold dread of the dark.",

The Spy Who Sang Too Late
Period Piece x Tragic Musical

Vienna, 1911. Retired imperial spymaster Auguste Vereaux has sworn off ciphers for a quiet life conducting the opera house orchestra. But when a coded assassination plot surfaces hidden inside an aria, Auguste discovers the conspiracy obeys the iron laws of tragic musical theater: every secret must be confessed in song, every villain gets exactly one heartbreaking solo, and no one survives the third act finale. To save the Archduke, Auguste must out-warble a baritone double agent — knowing that whoever hits the final high note dies for it.

Starring: Ex-Spy
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Ex-Spy stumbles into a Period Piece conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Tragic Musical.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered Edwardian spy in a tuxedo stands center-stage in a gilded opera house, one gloved hand raised mid-aria as a hidden pistol glints, golden stage light flooding around him.",
  "background": "The interior of a lavish Belle Époque opera house seen from the stage, ornate gilded balconies and crimson velvet boxes receding into shadowed depth. A vast painted ceiling and dimmed crystal chandelier loom overhead, dust and haze catching the spotlight. The orchestra pit glows faintly in the lower foreground.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, warm amber cinematic grade with deep shadows, photorealistic, volumetric haze, fine film grain",

The Velvet Congregation
Gothic Horror x Cult Exposé

Disgraced grifter Dorian Vane fakes his way into the Order of the Crimson Lamb — a candlelit hilltop commune of beautiful zealots — planning to swindle their tithe-rich coffers and slip out by dawn. But the documentary crew he hired as a cover starts capturing things their lenses shouldn't: doors that breathe, a charismatic prophetess who hasn't aged since 1612, and devotees who whisper Dorian's real name in their sleep. The longer he cons them, the more the manor seems to be conning him. By the third confessional interview, he can't tell if he's running the scam — or starring in the ritual.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Gothic Horror x Cult Exposé. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Con Artist.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sweating con man in a rumpled suit stands frozen in a candlelit gothic manor hall while robed cult figures watch from the shadows, shot like a prestige horror one-sheet.",
  "background": "The interior of a decaying gothic manor great hall at night, vaulted stone arches receding into velvet darkness, hundreds of dripping red candles lining the walls. Deep focus reveals a grand staircase swallowed by shadow and faint silhouettes of hooded figures standing motionless on the landing. Thin incense haze drifts through shafts of moonlight from a stained-glass window high above.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, low-key chiaroscuro lighting, desaturated crimson-and-charcoal cinematic grade, fine film grain, photorealistic, volumetric candle haze",
  "aesthetics": "dread, sensual, opulent decay, occult intimacy, prestige horror",
  "lighting": "Warm flickering candlelight rakes across faces from below while cold blue moonlight bleeds from a high window, leaving deep velvety shadows.",

The Graveyard Shift: A Bedside Manor
Mockumentary x Gothic Horror

A scrappy documentary crew thinks they're filming a feel-good piece about chronically understaffed Blackmoor Sanatorium — until they meet Sister Imelda Vane, the night nurse who hasn't aged since 1887 and insists the patients "check out, but they never leave." Between deadpan talking-head interviews and lingering candlelit glances, the crew slowly realizes the call buttons ring from empty beds and Imelda's "smoke break" is just her exhaling at a portrait of a dead duke. It's all very professional. It's also deeply, unsettlingly horny. As the sound guy starts sleepwalking toward the morgue, the crew must decide: get the Emmy, or get out alive.

Starring: Night-Shift Nurse
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Mockumentary x Gothic Horror. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Night-Shift Nurse.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal mock-documentary one-sheet of a pale, beautiful night-shift nurse standing in a crumbling Gothic hospital corridor, caught in a handheld camera's harsh on-camera light while shadows loom behind her.",
  "background": "A long decaying Victorian sanatorium corridor with peeling damask wallpaper, rusted wheelchairs, and rows of iron hospital beds vanishing into darkness. Flickering gas-lamp sconces line the walls and a single fogged window leaks cold moonlight at the far end. Dust and fine mist hang in the air, giving deep receding perspective and a sense of something waiting just beyond the frame.",

Personal Foul: Badge of Dishonor
Buddy Cop x Workplace Comedy

Disgraced ex-quarterback Roy "Hot Hands" Delgado — banned from the league for the infamous "fumble that broke a city's heart" — is sentenced to community service as a civilian liaison at the 14th Precinct's Records Department. Partnered with Detective Anita Vance, a sleep-deprived workaholic who communicates exclusively in passive-aggressive sticky notes, Roy must solve a corruption case while filing overtime forms in triplicate. The tension is professional. The tension is also, regrettably, not just professional. Between stakeouts and surprise HR seminars, they'll learn that justice is a team sport and the copier is jammed again.

Starring: Disgraced Athlete
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Buddy Cop x Workplace Comedy. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Disgraced Athlete.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal buddy-cop one-sheet of a chiseled disgraced athlete and a frazzled detective standing back-to-back in a fluorescent-lit office bullpen, weapons drawn, surrounded by paperwork chaos.",
  "background": "A cramped, beige precinct office bullpen lit by harsh overhead fluorescent tubes, rows of metal desks buried under teetering paper stacks and ringing rotary phones. A jammed photocopier spews pages into a drift on the floor in the mid-ground, while a motivational poster and a corkboard of overdue forms blur into the depth. The fluorescent glow gives everything a slightly oppressive, sweaty office sheen.",

Sworn In: A Field Study in Treason
Spy Thriller x Mockumentary

Idealistic young attorney Marisol "Mari" Vega thinks she's been hired to fight for the little guy — until a documentary crew, hired by an agency that doesn't officially exist, follows her through her first week at a law firm that's actually a CIA front. As she earnestly files pro bono motions, she keeps accidentally extracting state secrets, sleeping with at least two double agents, and signing what may or may not be a confession. The handheld interviews get sweatier and the briefcase gets heavier. Justice is blind, but the surveillance cameras are not.

Starring: Idealistic Lawyer
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Spy Thriller x Mockumentary. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Idealistic Lawyer.
Image prompt:
{

Deep one-point perspective down the hallway with a lone red EXIT sign and faint silhouettes of figures lurking at the far end. The Pale Crown of Buzzard Creek
Revisionist Western x Gothic Horror

Deposed boy-king Aldric Vane flees a coup with nothing but a tarnished crown, a borrowed Stetson, and a cough that won't quit. He rides into Buzzard Creek hoping to vanish — but the town's saloon is run by something centuries old that remembers what royal blood tastes like. As the gunslingers turn gray and the church bell tolls on its own, Aldric must learn to draw a revolver faster than he ever learned to bow. There are no heroes out here. Only a king, a curse, and a very thirsty congregation.

Starring: Exiled Royal
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Revisionist Western show had a baby with a Gothic Horror miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Exiled Royal.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A dust-caked exiled young king in a long oilskin coat stands in a haunted ghost-town main street at dusk, tarnished crown on his head and revolver at his hip, as a pale Gothic figure looms in the saloon doorway behind him.",
  "background": "A decaying frontier ghost town at blue-hour dusk, weathered timber storefronts leaning at sick angles down a fog-choked main street. A crooked church steeple punctures a bruised purple sky behind dead cottonwoods. Low ground mist curls over the dirt road and ravens scatter from a sagging gallows in the deep background.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated cold-teal and ember-orange cinematic grade, photorealistic, heavy volumetric haze, fine film grain",

Till Pursuit Do Us Part
Car-Chase Thriller x Marriage of Convenience

Burned-out ex-spy Dex Calloway needs a fake marriage to slip past a border checkpoint with a stolen ledger — so he weds his getaway driver, Nadia, in a drive-thru chapel doing 60mph. Now the most dangerous cartel in three countries is in the rearview, but the real crisis is that Nadia keeps "accidentally" rear-ending the agency that's tailing them, and Dex forgot their anniversary (it was Tuesday). They must dodge missiles, debate whose name goes on the joint passport, and discover that a marriage of convenience comes with very inconvenient feelings. Buckle up — and apologize.

Starring: Ex-Spy
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Car-Chase Thriller setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Marriage of Convenience. Our reluctant hero: a Ex-Spy.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A handsome ex-spy and his sharp-eyed wife brace inside a speeding muscle car mid-chase, wedding rings catching the light as a tangle of pursuing vehicles burns behind them.",
  "background": "A sun-scorched coastal highway at golden hour seen from a low chase angle, asphalt streaking with motion blur toward a hazy seaside skyline. Behind the hero car, three black SUVs and a flipped sedan trail smoke and sparks across the lanes. Heat shimmer and tire smoke drift into a dusty orange sky, with palm silhouettes whipping past at the road's edge.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, motion-blurred background, teal-orange cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric dust and lens flare",
  "aesthetics": "high-octane, glossy, romantic-tense, blockbuster, kinetic",
  "lighting": "warm low golden-hour sun raking through the windshield with hard rim light on the couple and fiery backlight from the wreckage behind.",
  "palette": [
    "#E8852A",
    "#1C3B4A",
    "#F2D6A2",
    "#7A1410",
    "#0C1418"
  ],
  "bg_brightness": 58,
  "elements": [
    {
      "type": "obj",
      "text": "",
      "desc": "A rugged ex-spy in a torn tuxedo and loosened bow tie gripping the wheel of a vintage muscle car, jaw clenched, gold wedding band visible on his hand, sunlight catching sweat on his brow, photoreal",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.3,
      "y": 0.3,
      "w": 0.42,
      "h": 0.46
    },
    {
      "type": "obj",
      "text": "",
      "desc": "An elegant woman in a windswept satin wedding dress in the passenger seat aiming a pistol out the window with one hand while clutching a stolen leather ledger, fierce determined expression, matching ring glinting, photoreal",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.04,
      "y": 0.34,
      "w": 0.34,
      "h": 0.44
    },
    {
      "type": "obj",
      "text": "",
      "desc": "Three black SUVs in tight pursuit on a smoking highway with sparks and debris, motion-blurred, seen receding behind the hero car, photoreal",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.0,
      "y": 0.58,
      "w": 1.0,
      "h": 0.3
    },
    {
      "type": "obj",
      "text": "",
      "desc": "Two interlocked gold wedding rings dangling from the rearview mirror, gleaming in the sunlight, foreground focus, photoreal",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.62,
      "y": 0.4,
      "w": 0.16,
      "h": 0.16
    },
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "TILL PURSUIT\nDO US PART",
      "desc": "Massive bold condensed sans-serif title in distressed metallic chrome with orange ember glow on the edges, stacked two lines, slight perspective tilt, dominating the lower third",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.06,
      "y": 0.74,
      "w": 0.88,
      "h": 0.16
    },
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "They said I do. They meant I drive.",
      "desc": "Small italic tagline in clean white serif, centered, directly above the title",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.18,
      "y": 0.69,
      "w": 0.64,
      "h": 0.04
    },
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "VELOCITY PICTURES PRESENTS  A RHEA TANDON FILM  \"TILL PURSUIT DO US PART\"  MARCUS DELANEY  SOPHIA VANCE  DIRECTED BY RHEA TANDON",
      "desc": "Tiny condensed all-caps billing block in pale gray, centered, single justified line at the very bottom",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.1,
      "y": 0.94,
      "w": 0.8,
      "h": 0.035
    },
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "IN THEATERS THIS SUMMER",
      "desc": "Small spaced-out white sans-serif release line just above the billing block",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.3,
      "y": 0.905,
      "w": 0.4,
      "h": 0.025
    }
  ]
}
A Lamp for the Drowning Heart
Period Romance x Dark Comedy

In 1887 on a storm-battered Cornish rock, lonesome lighthouse keeper Bartholomew Quill falls desperately in love with Cordelia, a shipwreck survivor he nurses back to health — until he realizes she keeps surviving shipwrecks, suspiciously, and that several of her previous "fiancés" are now lighthouse-shaped. As the romance curdles into a politely homicidal battle of wits, Bartholomew defends his beloved beacon, his dignity, and his nightly microwaved kipper supper. It's tender. It's deranged. Someone gets fed to a foghorn.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Period Romance in act one, Dark Comedy by act three, and a Lighthouse Keeper sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A windswept Victorian lighthouse keeper and a mysterious woman in period dress share a charged, ominous embrace on storm-lashed rocks beneath a towering lighthouse, lit by a single sweeping beam.",
  "background": "A jagged sea-cliff at dusk dominated by a tall white stone lighthouse, its beacon throwing a hard cone of light across roiling slate-grey waves. Cold spray and volumetric mist drift across the foreground while bruised storm clouds pile up behind. Distant splintered shipwreck timbers jut from the surf at the cliff base.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated steely-blue grade with warm beam highlights, photorealistic, fine film grain, volumetric haze, naturalistic skin tones",
  "aesthetics": "gothic period romance, ominous, darkly comic, windswept, opulent yet bleak",
  "lighting": "a single rotating lighthouse beam rakes the couple from the side while cold ambient dusk fills the shadows and sea spray catches the light.",

The Last Embalmer: Tape 7
Post-Apocalyptic x Found Footage

Mort Kessler ran the only funeral home left standing after the world ended, and he refuses to bury anyone without proper paperwork — even when civilization itself is the corpse. Armed with a dying camcorder and an unshakable belief in "dignity for the deceased," he documents his rounds through the ash-choked ruins, narrating each body's eulogy to a microphone nobody will ever hear. But the dead in the wasteland have stopped staying buried, and Mort's footage captures something following him home. This is the tape they found in his hearse. It is the only thing that survived.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Undertaker, alone, in the rain. The genre? Post-Apocalyptic. The vibe? Unmistakably Found Footage. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal found-footage one-sheet of a weary undertaker filming himself in a rain-lashed post-apocalyptic graveyard, shot through a grimy camcorder viewfinder overlay.",
  "background": "A ruined cemetery at dusk on the edge of a collapsed city, leaning headstones half-swallowed by ash and weeds, the broken silhouette of a dead skyline smoldering on the horizon. Heavy rain streaks down through a sodium-orange haze, and a wrecked black hearse sits abandoned in the muddy middle distance. The depth recedes into smoky gray gloom punctuated by a single distant fire glow.",
  "art_style": "shot on a degraded consumer camcorder, handheld VHS-grain found-footage look, photorealistic, shallow depth of field, low-light noise, lens flecked with rain droplets, desaturated cold grade with sodium highlights, volumetric haze",
  "aesthetics": "grim, claustrophobic, documentary-real, decayed, dread-soaked, lo-fi",
  "lighting": "A harsh on-camera LED light blasts the undertaker's wet face from below while everything behind him falls into cold rain-soaked darkness.",
  "palette": [
