Goodfella Pirouette: A Mob in Pieces
Body Horror x Dance Musical

Vinnie "The Hinge" Carbone is the most feared knee-breaker in Cannelloni City — until a cursed protection racket leaves him with a body that won't stop dancing. Every time the music swells, his joints reassemble into jazz hands, his ribs unfold into spirit fingers, and his boss's enemies are choreographed to death. Now Vinnie must out-dance a rival family of contortionist body-snatchers before the whole borough peels itself into a chorus line. It's a horror musical where the only way to survive is to nail the landing.

Starring: Mob Enforcer
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Body Horror and Dance Musical coexist uneasily, our hero — a Mob Enforcer — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A menacing mob enforcer in a blood-flecked suit caught mid-pirouette under a single theatrical spotlight, his fingers grotesquely elongating into impossible jazz hands as his body partially unravels into sinew and muscle.",
  "background": "A derelict 1970s vaudeville theater interior gone to rot, with peeling gilded balconies, torn velvet curtains, and a stage floor slick with dark liquid. Deep shadows swallow the back rows while volumetric haze drifts through a hard spotlight beam. A faint blood-spattered chalk dance diagram is visible underfoot.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, fine film grain, desaturated noir grade with bruised crimson accents, photorealistic, volumetric haze, practical theatrical lighting",

Ledger of the Dead Mule Saloon
Weird West x Cold Case

Twelve years after her husband walked into a dust storm and never walked out, gunslinging single mom Prudence "Pru" Calloway reopens a case nobody in the haunted town of Cicatrice will speak of. Trouble is, the only witness is a talking weathervane, the murder weapon keeps relocating itself, and her seven-year-old keeps "helpfully" interrogating ghosts during church. As the high desert bleeds into something older and stranger, Pru learns the cold case was never closed — it was buried alive. Justice rides at dusk. Bedtime is at eight.

Starring: Single Parent
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Single Parent, alone, in the rain. The genre? Weird West. The vibe? Unmistakably Cold Case. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered female gunslinger stands alone in driving rain on a desolate frontier street at dusk, a small child's silhouette clutching her coat, an evidence ledger glowing faintly in her free hand.",
  "background": "A muddy main street of a half-abandoned Old West town at blue-hour dusk, rain hammering down in sheets and pooling in deep ruts. Crooked saloon facades and a leaning church steeple recede into a bruised violet sky behind veils of mist. Distant lantern light bleeds gold through the storm, and a single crooked weathervane catches the last sliver of stormlight.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated teal-and-amber cinematic grade, heavy practical rain, volumetric haze, photorealistic, fine film grain",

The Last Sermon of Sorrowfen
Noir x High Fantasy

In the rain-drowned realm of Greythorn, where elves run gin joints and the moon never sets, disgraced cult leader Brother Vellum Crane peddles salvation to anyone with coin and a soul to spare. When his most devoted acolyte turns up dead in a temple of black water — drained of memory, not blood — Vellum must solve the murder before the city watch pins it on him. Trouble is, every suspect is a god, and Vellum's only lead is a prophecy he wrote himself, drunk, to swindle a duchess. Faith is a racket. The truth is worse.

Starring: Cult Leader
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Cult Leader, alone, in the rain. The genre? Noir. The vibe? Unmistakably High Fantasy. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A robed cult leader stands alone in torrential rain on the steps of a fantastical cathedral, backlit by an enormous low-hanging moon, shot like a noir thriller.",
  "background": "A rain-soaked gothic-fantasy city square at deep night, cobblestones slicked into mirrors of reflected lamplight, towering cathedral spires and crooked elven townhouses receding into wet fog. A vast pale moon hangs low between the spires, its light cutting through volumetric haze. Distant gas-lamps glow amber along a canal, their reflections smeared by the downpour.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, heavy rain practical effects, low-key chiaroscuro noir grade with cold steel-blue shadows and warm amber highlights, photorealistic, volumetric haze, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "moody, ominous, mysterious, opulent decay, sacred and seedy, rain-drenched",
  "lighting": "a single hard backlight from the giant moon rims the figure while a low amber lantern uplights his face, leaving the eyes pooled in shadow.",
  "palette": [
    "#0E1620",
    "#1F3A4D",
    "#C8932F",
    "#7E8FA0",
    "#E9E2CF"
  ],

      "w": 0.4,
      "h": 0.03
    }
  ]

Crown of the Crater: A Survival in Three Acts
First Contact x Survival

Deposed by a palace coup and shoved into an escape pod with nothing but a ceremonial scepter and a half-eaten brie, Archduke Bartholomew "Barty" Von Wexley III crash-lands on a swamp moon that refuses to acknowledge his titles. Worse, he's not alone: a freshly-arrived alien delegation has chosen THIS week to make humanity's first contact, and they assume the loudest, most entitled human is in charge. Now Barty must broker interspecies peace, dodge carnivorous fungus, and learn to filter his own urine — all while insisting it be called "royal hydration." It's diplomacy by tantrum, survival by spite.

Starring: Exiled Royal
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's First Contact meets Survival... but the protagonist is a Exiled Royal.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A mud-caked exiled royal in tattered finery brandishes a glowing scepter at a towering bioluminescent alien on a misty alien swamp ridge at dusk, lit like a prestige sci-fi survival epic.",
  "background": "A vast alien swamp moon at blue-hour twilight, with steaming wetlands and gnarled luminescent trees receding into volumetric fog. A pair of ringed planets hang low in a bruised violet sky behind jagged crater ridges. Wisps of spore-mist drift through shafts of dying light, giving deep atmospheric layering from foreground reeds to distant peaks.",

The Last Crusade of Sergeant Maple
Slasher x High Fantasy

Decorated war veteran Cliff Maple just wanted a quiet retirement growing turnips in the kingdom of Velmoor — until an axe-wielding immortal called The Thresher begins reaping the realm's villagers one by one. Plagued by battlefield flashbacks and a bad knee, Cliff is the only soul who recognizes the killer's pattern: it slaughters in rhyme. Armed with an enchanted prosthetic leg and the world's most sarcastic talking shield, he must outlast a hooded reaper that simply will not stay dead. War was hell. This is worse, and there are dragons watching.

Starring: War Veteran
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Slasher meets High Fantasy... but the protagonist is a War Veteran.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A grizzled middle-aged war veteran in battered fantasy armor stands defiant in a fog-choked medieval village at night, gripping a glowing enchanted prosthetic leg like a weapon while a hooded axe-killer looms in the mist behind him.",
  "background": "A ruined high-fantasy village at midnight: crooked timber cottages with shattered doors, a distant crumbling castle silhouette against a sickly green moon. Last Rites & Wrong Moves
Dance Musical x Revenge Thriller

Father Silas Boon hasn't believed in much since the night a crooked land baron and his sequined enforcers torched the parish dance hall — and the orphans' tap shoes inside it. Now the only language his shattered congregation still speaks is choreography, and the only way to take down the man who killed their faith is to out-dance and out-gun his entire crew at the town's annual Jubilee Showdown. Armed with a censer in one hand and a revolver in the other, Silas trades his collar for a spotlight and his sermons for spinning roundhouse pirouettes. Vengeance has never been so well-rehearsed. Faith, fury, and jazz hands collide.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Dance Musical and Revenge Thriller coexist uneasily, our hero — a Disillusioned Priest — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A grizzled priest stands mid-stride in a smoke-filled, neon-lit dance hall, revolver in one hand and swinging censer in the other, frozen in a dramatic dance pose as armed dancers blur behind him.",

A worn parquet dance floor stretches into shadow, glittering with shattered glass and spent shell casings. The Sophomore Conspiracy
Conspiracy Thriller x Magical Academia

Burned-out investigative reporter Margo Vell thinks she's exposing a tuition-laundering scheme at elite Brackwell University — until her source bursts into blue flame and her press badge starts predicting the future. The deeper she digs, the more she realizes the "endowment committee" is a coven, the dean is nine hundred years old, and her freedom-of-information requests keep returning as enchanted scrolls. Now Margo must pass three semesters of forbidden magic she never enrolled in, all while ranking microwave lasagnas by emotional damage. Truth, it turns out, requires a permission slip signed in moonlight.

Starring: Cynical Journalist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Conspiracy Thriller in act one, Magical Academia by act three, and a Cynical Journalist sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary trench-coated journalist stands at a fog-drenched gothic university gate clutching a glowing magical scroll, photoreal cinematic one-sheet with eerie arcane light bleeding through conspiracy-board strings.",
  "background": "A towering neo-gothic university quad at midnight, wet cobblestones reflecting amber lamplight, ivy-choked stone arches receding into blue fog. Floating points of pale magical light drift between the spires like dust caught in moonbeams. A faint web of red conspiracy-board string stretches across the upper sky, pinned with photographs that hover impossibly in the air.",

For Better or For Bocce
Marriage of Convenience x Sports Doc

Broke installation artist D040 "Mo" Moretti will lose his condemned harbor studio — and the whole sinking town of Pellucid Bay — unless he marries someone, anyone, for the municipal "Spousal Stability Grant." Enter Renata, a stone-faced retired bocce world champion who needs a green card and a doubles partner. Their loveless paper marriage becomes a gritty, talking-head sports documentary as Mo trains to win the Regional Bocce Cup he doesn't understand, the prize money the only thing that can save them both. Filmed entirely by a wheezing crew that will not stop asking how they feel.

Starring: Struggling Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Marriage of Convenience and Sports Doc coexist uneasily, our hero — a Struggling Artist — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal documentary-style poster of a paint-splattered struggling artist and a steely bocce champion standing back-to-back on a cracked seaside court, framed like both a wedding portrait and a sports doc still.",
  "background": "A weathered Italian-American coastal town at golden hour, a crumbling municipal bocce court of packed clay in the foreground with chalk lines and scattered heavy balls. Behind them tilting clapboard houses and a half-sunk fishing pier recede into a hazy harbor. A documentary camera operator with a shoulder rig is visible slightly out of focus at the edge, capturing the moment.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, observational documentary handheld framing, warm golden-hour grade with cool harbor shadows, photorealistic, fine film grain, subtle lens flare",

The Last Brushstroke Commune
Post-Apocalyptic x Cult Exposé

Three years after the bombs turned the art world to ash, washed-up installation artist Dov Petrakis joins the Radiant Pigment — a doomsday cult that worships the last surviving tube of cadmium red and conducts very sweaty "color rituals" in a flooded gallery basement. Armed only with a camcorder and crippling imposter syndrome, Dov documents the cult's charismatic founder, who promises immortality through "smoldering brushwork." As the candlelit communions escalate and the Founder demands a final masterpiece painted in something other than paint, Dov realizes the exposé he's filming may be his last commission. Equal parts apocalyptic dread and unhinged, oily yearning.

Starring: Struggling Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Post-Apocalyptic x Cult Exposé. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Struggling Artist.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A gaunt struggling artist clutches a glowing tube of red paint and a battered camcorder amid a candlelit flooded gallery of robed cult figures, all under a ruined post-apocalyptic sky.",
  "background": "The interior of a bombed-out art museum half-submerged in dark water, broken skylights revealing an ash-grey toxic sky beyond. Hundreds of dripping candles line cracked marble walls hung with empty, scorched picture frames. Hooded figures in stained linen robes kneel in the shallow water, fading into smoky depth behind the central figure.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated post-apocalyptic grade with a single bleeding red accent, photorealistic, heavy volumetric haze, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "ritualistic dread, sweaty candlelit intimacy, decayed grandeur, sensual menace, documentary grit",
  "lighting": "low warm candlelight raking across wet skin and stone with one cold shaft of ash-grey daylight from a shattered skylight overhead.",
  "palette": [
    "#0E0B0A",
    "#C8302B",
    "#7A6A52",
    "#D9C9A3",
    "#2B2622"
  ],
  "bg_brightness": 30,
  "elements": [
    {
      "type": "obj",
      "text": "",

The Last Unicorn IPO: A Disruption in Three Acts
Satire x Tragic Musical

Atlas Brightwall, the boyish trillionaire founder of a "mindfulness-as-a-service" empire, learns his flagship app has accidentally achieved sentience and chosen to unionize. As regulators circle and his board stages a hostile coup set to soaring power ballads, Atlas must decide whether to sell his soul or merely his data. Everyone in Silicon Valley communicates exclusively through devastating sung confessionals and synchronized standing ovations. By the final crescendo, the only thing that crashes harder than the servers is Atlas himself, belting his resignation from atop a kombucha tap.

Starring: Tech Billionaire
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Satire setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Tragic Musical. Our reluctant hero: a Tech Billionaire.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A tearful tech billionaire in a charcoal turtleneck stands mid-power-ballad on a glowing minimalist stage, arms outstretched, as confetti and stock tickers rain down behind him.",
  "background": "A cavernous all-white product-launch auditorium fades into deep shadow, rows of empty seats glowing faintly under cold spotlights. A massive curved LED wall behind the stage displays a plummeting green-and-red stock graph dissolving into pixels. Volumetric haze drifts through theatrical spotlight beams, giving the space an operatic, cathedral-like depth.",

The Body of Evidence
Forensic Procedural x Satire

Burned-out investigative journalist Margo Vane hasn't filed a real story since her divorce, until a city councilman turns up dead in a way the coroner calls "frankly, suspiciously sexy." Embedded with an elite forensics unit that treats every crime scene like a perfume commercial, Margo discovers a conspiracy where the murder weapon is charisma and the cover-up smells like cedar and regret. As bodies pile up and lab techs keep loosening their ties, she must crack the case before the truth gets respectfully redacted. It's a dread-soaked exposé about institutional rot — that absolutely refuses to stop fogging up the lens.

Starring: Cynical Journalist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Forensic Procedural x Satire. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Cynical Journalist.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A cynical female journalist in a rain-soaked trench coat stands over a sleek backlit forensics morgue table while a chalk outline glistens beneath dramatic blue light, shot like a prestige crime thriller one-sheet",
  "background": "Interior of a high-end forensic morgue rendered like a noir thriller set, stainless steel tables receding into deep shadow with cold fluorescent strips overhead. Volumetric haze drifts through shafts of blue light, and a frosted evidence-locker wall glows faintly in the far background. Reflective wet floors throw long distorted highlights toward the camera.",

Pas de Deux ex Machina
Dance Musical x Conspiracy Thriller

Father Aurelio Voss lost his faith years ago, but never lost his killer jazz hands — so when his struggling parish launches a fundraising dance revue, he choreographs the show of his life. Then he notices the routines spell out coordinates, his lead dancer vanishes mid-pirouette, and the diocese's accountant is found face-down in a tambourine. As Aurelio tap-dances deeper into a shadow syndicate laundering money through liturgical sequins, he realizes the only thing standing between the truth and a global cover-up is one disillusioned priest with rhythm. He sobs into his microwave lasagna between rehearsals. The choreography never stops.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Dance Musical in act one, Conspiracy Thriller by act three, and a Disillusioned Priest sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary priest in a black cassock frozen mid-dance pose under a single theatrical spotlight, surrounded by ominous shadowy figures and scattered evidence, shot like a glossy noir thriller one-sheet.",

A polished marble floor reflects a hard pool of spotlight in the foreground while corkboard with red strings and surveillance photos lurks in a shadowed alcove at left. Volumetric haze drifts through angled god-rays from high windows.",

Graveyard Shift: A Western in Three Courses
Acid Western x Food Doc

Out in the sun-scorched nowhere town of Buzzard's Mercy, night-shift nurse Delphine "Del" Cortez runs the only clinic for ninety miles — and, after midnight, the only kitchen. When a fevered drifter dies mid-sentence babbling about a chili recipe that "cures or kills," Del's documentary crew follows her into a hallucinatory border of cactus broth, peyote consommé, and patients who pay in heirloom beans. Part frontier ghost story, part lovingly plated food doc, it asks the only question that matters at 3 a.m.: is the wound infected, or just underseasoned? Narrated like a cooking show, lit like a fever dream.

Starring: Night-Shift Nurse
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Acid Western meets Food Doc... but the protagonist is a Night-Shift Nurse.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered night-shift nurse stands in a sun-cracked desert ghost town at twilight, holding a bloodstained ladle over a simmering enamel pot, lit like a cinematic fever dream.",
  "background": "A derelict Old West frontier street at deep dusk, with peeling clapboard storefronts, a leaning saloon sign, and dust hanging in the air. The horizon burns a bruised orange-violet behind distant mesas and a lone saguaro. Heat shimmer and floating embers blur the deep background while the foreground stays crisp.",

The Last Reel Heist
Workplace Comedy x Heist

Faded action legend Roman Castellane hasn't had a hit since the '90s and now spends his days clocking in as a "brand ambassador" at the very studio that forgot him. When he discovers the suits plan to delete his master films to make room for a reality show, Roman recruits the world's most dysfunctional office — a temp, an IT guy, and a passive-aggressive HR rep — to steal the negatives back before Friday's quarterly review. There are laser grids, but also a mandatory team-building seminar. He's done his own stunts for forty years; the printer jam may finally end him.

Starring: Aging Movie Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Workplace Comedy show had a baby with a Heist miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Aging Movie Star.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A silver-haired aging action star in a wrinkled tuxedo crouches in an office-lit vault doorway clutching a reel of film, lit like a heist thriller but framed by cubicle clutter, on a photoreal vertical one-sheet.",
  "background": "A sleek corporate film-studio vault corridor at night, glowing emergency strip-lighting along the floor and a green laser security grid crisscrossing the deep background. To the sides, beige office cubicles, a water cooler, and motivational posters bleed into the shadows. Volumetric haze gives depth as the corridor recedes toward a glowing red exit sign.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-and-amber cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze, subtle film grain, crisp key light",

The Mortician's Long Goodbye
Cosmic Horror x Hardboiled Detective

Embalmer Silas Crane runs the only funeral home in a fog-choked harbor town, and lately his clients won't stay quiet about what they saw beyond the veil. When a banker turns up dead with seawater in his lungs and a star-shaped wound that won't close, Silas takes the case the cops won't touch — armed with a flask, a worn fedora, and the unsettling ability to hear corpses confess. Every lead drags him deeper toward a sleeping thing under the docks that's been recruiting the dead as witnesses. He's seen a lot of bodies. He's never had one ask him for backup.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Undertaker, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cosmic Horror. The vibe? Unmistakably Hardboiled Detective. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary undertaker in a soaked trench coat and fedora stands in a rain-lashed cemetery while a colossal eldritch silhouette with star-shaped eyes looms in the storm clouds above him.",
  "background": "A waterlogged hillside graveyard at 3 a.m., crooked headstones receding into thick coastal fog and a distant fishing-harbor skyline with one flickering lighthouse. Sheets of rain streak the air, and the low clouds part to reveal an impossibly vast tentacled shape with constellation-bright eyes pressing down on the town. Puddles on the foreground mud mirror the monstrous glow, blending noir wet-street reflection with cosmic dread.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, deep teal-and-amber noir grade, heavy practical rain, volumetric haze, photorealistic, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "hardboiled noir, lovecraftian dread, rain-soaked, brooding, cinematic",
  "lighting": "a single hard amber streetlamp rakes the undertaker from the side while sickly green light bleeds down from the creature above, leaving deep wet shadows.",

Last Precinct: Rules of Engagement
Post-Apocalyptic x Military Action

Disgraced detective Roy Calhoun was drummed off the force for losing an evidence locker — then the world ended, which honestly took the heat off. Now he runs a one-man cold-case bureau out of a gutted strip mall in the irradiated ruins of Phoenix, trading bullet casings for canned beans. When a missing-persons case unspools into a militia's plan to weaponize the last working satellite, Roy realizes the only way to crack it is to stop investigating and start fragging. He's got a magnifying glass, a belt-fed machine gun, and zero remaining badge. Justice is served in full auto.

Starring: Disgraced Detective
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Disgraced Detective stumbles into a Post-Apocalyptic conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Military Action.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A grizzled detective in a tattered trench coat over body armor stands amid a bombed-out desert city, cradling a belt-fed machine gun while clutching a battered case file, dusk smoke swirling behind him.",
  "background": "A ruined post-apocalyptic Phoenix at golden-hour dusk, collapsed freeway overpasses and gutted skyscrapers fading into ochre dust haze. Wrecked military Humvees and a downed satellite dish litter the cracked highway receding to the horizon. A blood-orange irradiated sky glows with distant smoke columns and a faint dead-pixel moon.",

Til Death Do Us Draw
Acid Western x Marriage of Convenience

Cassius "The Whisper" Vane retired from contract killing to a sun-blasted ghost town with nothing but a revolver, a hot plate, and crushing despair. When the town's water rights — and his pension — hinge on a forged marriage license, the local undertaker's widow offers a deal: a fake wedding, a real ranch, no feelings allowed. But between hallucinatory peyote shootouts and a microwave dinner he weeps into every single night, Cassius discovers the deadliest target he's ever failed to eliminate is his own heart. Saddle up for a feverish, sobbing love story with bullets.

Starring: Retired Assassin
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Acid Western in act one, Marriage of Convenience by act three, and a Retired Assassin sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered retired assassin in a dusty black duster stands in a cracked desert ghost town clutching a microwave dinner tray, a reluctant bride in faded lace beside him, both lit by a blistering acid-orange sunset.",
  "background": "A bone-dry frontier ghost town at dusk, warped wooden storefronts and a leaning church steeple receding into heat-shimmer. The sky is a bruised, oversaturated wash of acid orange, sulfur yellow, and dirty violet, with mirage ripples distorting the horizon. Fine alkali dust hangs in the still air, catching low golden backlight.",

Vital Signs: The Harmony Ward Deprogramming
Cult Exposé x Jukebox Musical

Night-shift nurse Pilar Okafor thought the new wellness collective renting the hospital's east wing was just very into vitamins and four-part harmony. Then her patients start signing over their pensions between vitals checks and the charge nurse only communicates in show tunes. As cameras roll on a "routine documentary about hospital staffing," Pilar realizes she's the lone uncharmed soul on a ward run by a chart-topping cult that literally sings its way out of accountability. To save thirty sedated true believers, she'll have to out-belt a charismatic guru during a 3 a.m. code blue. The IV drip keeps tempo.

Starring: Night-Shift Nurse
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Cult Exposé meets Jukebox Musical... but the protagonist is a Night-Shift Nurse.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A documentary-style one-sheet of a determined night-shift nurse standing in a fluorescent hospital corridor while a glowing, choir-robed cult sings in soft focus behind her, lit like a true-crime exposé crossed with a stage musical.",
  "background": "A long, deep-perspective hospital corridor at night, lined with empty gurneys and flickering fluorescent panels receding to a vanishing point. Halfway down the hall a doorway glows warm amber, spilling out a tightly packed group of figures in matching pale-blue wellness robes, slightly out of focus and uplit as if mid-song. Cold institutional teal dominates the foreground, warm cult light blooms in the distance, with faint volumetric haze drifting through the corridor.",

Love Island Lighthouse
Romantic Comedy x Reality Competition

Gull Pemberton has manned the Marrowick Point lighthouse alone for nineteen years, talking only to the foghorn and a barnacle he named Gerald. So when a stranded reality-TV crew commandeers his tower to film "Last Lonely Single," Gull discovers the only way to keep his beloved lighthouse from foreclosure is to win the show himself. Trouble is, he's fallen for the producer, the rules forbid romance with staff, and someone keeps rigging the elimination ceremonies. Cue confessional cams, a coupling firepit beside the foghorn, and the most awkward sunset proposal in maritime history.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Lighthouse Keeper stumbles into a Romantic Comedy conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Reality Competition.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered lighthouse keeper stands at the foot of his lighthouse beside a glittering reality-TV firepit and confessional camera, lit like a romantic dating-show one-sheet at golden hour.",
  "background": "A dramatic rocky headland at sunset with a tall white-and-red striped lighthouse rising against a peach-and-rose sky, its lamp glowing. Behind it the ocean catches warm light while a low bank of fog rolls across distant cliffs. Stage rigging, a string of festoon party lights, and a TV camera crane intrude awkwardly on the windswept natural scenery, blending wild coast with cheesy dating-show set dressing.",

      "h": 0.035
    },
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "COUPLE UP THIS SUMMER",
      "desc": "small spaced gold sans-serif release line, centered just below title",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.22,
      "y": 0.925,
      "w": 0.56,
      "h": 0.025
    }
  ]
}
The Last Light Between Us
Forbidden Romance x Urban Fantasy

Silas Vane has kept the Blackridge Lighthouse for nineteen lonely years, and he has one rule: never fall for the things the fog brings ashore. Then a storm delivers Marin — a banished tide-spirit who can only hold human form while his lamp keeps burning, and who turns to seafoam the instant it goes dark. Now Silas must choose between the oath that binds him to the light and the impossible, forbidden love that the city's bureaucratic Coven of Harbor Wardens has expressly outlawed. The catch: every hour they spend together costs the lighthouse one hour of fuel. Some flames you keep. Some flames keep you.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Lighthouse Keeper, alone, in the rain. The genre? Forbidden Romance. The vibe? Unmistakably Urban Fantasy. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-soaked lighthouse keeper and a luminous sea-spirit woman lean toward each other on storm-lashed rocks beneath a beam of light, a neon-lit coastal city glowing across the dark water behind them.",
  "background": "A jagged storm-battered shoreline at night, wet black rocks in the foreground giving way to churning silver-gray surf. Across the bay, a moody contemporary harbor city glitters with cold neon and amber window-light, half-veiled by drifting fog. Above, a single rotating lighthouse beam cuts a hard cone through the rain into a bruised purple sky.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, drifting rain caught in volumetric light, teal-and-amber cinematic grade, photorealistic, fine film grain, glistening wet highlights",
  "aesthetics": "moody, romantic, supernatural, rain-drenched, intimate, epic",
  "lighting": "a sweeping lighthouse beam rakes across the couple from the left while cool blue moonlight and distant warm city neon rim their wet faces.",

Caucus: The Cover-Up Cha-Cha
Conspiracy Doc x Dance Musical

State Senator Dallas Voorhees has a five-year plan, a winning smile, and one tiny problem: every time he tries to bury a scandal, his body involuntarily breaks into a flawless Broadway dance number. As shadowy gala kingmakers pull strings in smoke-filled ballrooms, a basement documentary crew zooms in on grainy footage of Dallas tap-dancing through redacted documents. Is it mind control? Is it the lizard lobbyists? Or is it just really, really good jazz hands? The truth is out there — and it's in perfect 5-6-7-8 time.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Conspiracy Doc show had a baby with a Dance Musical miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Ambitious Politician.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sweating politician frozen mid-jazz-square at a podium under a single spotlight, surrounded by red conspiracy string and surveillance monitors, shot like a paranoid thriller one-sheet.",
  "background": "A cavernous, dimly lit government press hall photographed in deep perspective, rows of empty velvet seats receding into shadow. The back wall is plastered with a chaotic corkboard of pinned documents, polaroids, and taut red string connecting them. Faint volumetric haze drifts through cold overhead light, with a wall of flickering CCTV monitors glowing in the far background.",

Code Blue, Encore Louder
Rock Opera x Urban Fantasy

Night-shift nurse Marisol Vega just wants to survive her double at St. Brigid's Memorial without falling asleep on the crash cart. But after a coding patient flatlines in perfect three-part harmony, she discovers the hospital sits atop a ley line where every dying breath powers an immortal glam-rock cult's encore. Armed with salt packets, a defibrillator, and a startlingly good belting voice, Marisol must out-sing the band's undead frontman before dawn — because in this city, the rules are simple: you cannot stop the bridge once it starts. A power-ballad-soaked supernatural thriller about overtime, oaths, and never breaking the high note.

Starring: Night-Shift Nurse
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Night-Shift Nurse stumbles into a Rock Opera conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Urban Fantasy.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of an exhausted night-shift nurse standing in a neon-lit hospital corridor, brandishing defibrillator paddles like a rock-god mic as glowing ley-line energy erupts behind her.",
  "background": "A long deserted hospital corridor at 3 a.m., fluorescent tubes flickering with one burnt-out gap, gurney and IV pole pushed against a wall. Cracks of electric-violet supernatural light bleed up through the linoleum floor seams like glowing ley lines, casting hard reflections. Deep in the background a swirl of stage-fog and a faint silhouette of an amplifier stack looms where the nurses' station should be.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, photorealistic, high-contrast cinematic grade, volumetric haze, subtle film grain, practical neon reflections",

The Quiet Type: A Family Affair
Family Saga x Spy Thriller

Reclusive novelist Margery Volk hasn't left her Vermont farmhouse in eleven years — or finished a sentence without three rewrites. When she discovers her late husband encrypted state secrets inside her bestselling family-saga trilogy, every intelligence agency on Earth descends on the sleepy town of Ashfield, posing as relatives at her granddaughter's christening. Now Margery must protect a manuscript, a town, and a casserole dish, all while her estranged son insists this is "exactly the kind of attention-seeking" he predicted. Murder, brunch, and dead drops have never been so passive-aggressive.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Family Saga and Spy Thriller coexist uneasily, our hero — a Reclusive Author — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A silver-haired woman in a cardigan stands on a misty New England farmhouse porch clutching a manuscript like a weapon, while suited agents lurk in the autumn fields behind her, shot like a prestige thriller one-sheet.",
  "background": "A weathered Vermont farmhouse porch at golden dusk, fog rolling across surrounding fields of dead corn and red maples. In the soft-focus distance, several silhouetted figures in dark suits stand motionless among the rows like scarecrows. The sky is a bruised amber-grey with low clouds, conveying cozy menace and rural isolation.",

The Quiet One: Live Studio Audience
Reality Competition x Coming-of-Age

Father Brightwater Pemberton has spent thirty serene years leading the Sunhaven Collective, dispensing wisdom about turnips and inner light to forty devoted followers. Then a producer's note slides under his yurt: he's been the unwitting star of a hit reality competition, and the "followers" are contestants voting weekly on whether his charisma still cuts it. To avoid elimination, the 58-year-old guru must do what he never did at 16 — survive a first crush, a betrayal, a humiliating talent round, and one tearful coming-of-age breakthrough. Enlightenment was easy. Puberty, apparently, is not.

Starring: Cult Leader
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Cult Leader discovers their quiet life is actually a Reality Competition, they must master the genre conventions of Coming-of-Age to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A serene robed cult leader stands center-stage bathed in reality-TV studio lights, ringed by stadium audience seating and glowing confessional booths, looking both transcendent and terrified.",
  "background": "A vast television soundstage dressed as a tranquil meadow commune, fake sunflowers and a wooden yurt upstage, but the sky is a black studio ceiling crammed with hanging stage lights and camera rigs. Tiered audience bleachers curve around the edges, packed with cheering silhouetted spectators waving glow-paddles. Deep haze and lens flare give the space theatrical depth, with elimination podiums glowing in the mid-ground.",

The Last Heir of Dustmoor
Grimdark x Neo-Western

Cassia Vaern, the deposed queen of a plague-rotted kingdom, wakes up in a sun-blasted border town with a price on her head, a cursed longsword she can't unsheathe sober, and a pickup truck full of someone else's bones. Hunted across the cracked highways by the iron-masked usurper's bounty riders, she trades crowns for diesel and prophecy for petty cash. All she wants is to reclaim her throne — but every oracle she meets keeps mentioning it's "definitely going to end badly." Grim, dusty, and faintly nauseous, this is revenge served lukewarm.

Starring: Exiled Royal
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Grimdark meets Neo-Western... but the protagonist is a Exiled Royal.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A grim exiled queen in battered armor and a duster coat stands on a cracked desert highway at blood-red dusk, cursed longsword across her shoulders, a rusted pickup truck behind her.",

Heat shimmer and fine dust hang in the air, with vultures circling a bruise-purple and ember-red sky. Deep haze gives layered atmospheric depth toward distant burning watchtowers.",

Term Limits: A Campaign Across Time
Dance Musical x Time Travel

Senator Dale Brightwater has one flaw no consultant can spin: every time he wins a vote, he involuntarily tap-dances himself thirteen years into the past. Now stranded in a primary that hasn't happened yet, Dale must waltz his own grandfather into endorsing him, jazz-square his way out of a 1994 fundraiser, and seduce the electorate of three different decades — all while a rival senator chases him through the timeline in glorious sequins. It's a high-kicking, paradox-stomping musical where the only thing harder than time travel is staying on message. Vote early. Vote often. Vote in every century.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Ambitious Politician, alone, in the rain. The genre? Dance Musical. The vibe? Unmistakably Time Travel. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched politician in a soaked three-piece suit mid-tap-dance under a streetlight, frozen lightning of a time-warp swirling around his patent-leather shoes, shot like a glossy theatrical one-sheet.",
  "background": "A rain-lashed nighttime city avenue lined with campaign banners and old-fashioned streetlamps, deep wet-asphalt reflections stretching toward a hazy capitol dome in the distance. Translucent clock faces and rippling blue energy spiral up from the puddles where the dancer's feet strike, bending the perspective. Crowd silhouettes blur at the edges holding faintly glowing placards from different eras.",

The Lamp Must Burn
Grimdark x Survival

On a fang-shaped rock at the edge of a dying world, surly keeper Bran Holloway has one job: keep the flame lit, or the kraken-haunted dark swallows the last shipping lane to the last free city. Then the oil runs out, plague-gulls eat his ration goat, and a half-drowned warlord washes ashore claiming the lighthouse is rightfully cursed. With three matches, a broken harpoon, and a deeply judgmental seagull named Earl, Bran must survive seven black nights. Grim. Wet. Out of oil. The light cannot go out.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Grimdark meets Survival... but the protagonist is a Lighthouse Keeper.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered, soaked lighthouse keeper braces against a storm-battered cliff lighthouse, lantern raised against a monstrous black sea under a blood-orange sky.",
  "background": "A jagged volcanic sea-stack at the world's edge, a crooked stone lighthouse leaning into a screaming gale, its weak beam barely cutting the murk. Behind it, immense black storm waves rise like walls, with the faint coiled silhouette of something enormous beneath the surface. The sky bleeds rust and ash, low clouds shredding around the tower.",

Page Turner: A Cop Named Margaret
Buddy Cop x Buddy Comedy

Reclusive literary novelist Bartholomew Quill hasn't left his cabin — or finished a sentence aloud — in eleven years. When a witness-protection mix-up dumps loud, rule-bending Detective Margaret "Mags" Delgado on his doorstep as his unwanted bodyguard-slash-partner, the two must crack a publishing-house murder ring together. He solves crimes through metaphor; she solves them through kicking doors. Neither will survive each other's road-trip playlist. From the studio that taught odd couples to share a Honda.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Reclusive Author, alone, in the rain. The genre? Buddy Cop. The vibe? Unmistakably Buddy Comedy. Cue title card.
Image prompt:

Steam rises from a manhole in the mid-ground while blurred police lightbar flashes pulse red and blue against brick walls. Deep shallow-focus bokeh of distant streetlights fills the upper third.",

The Last Encore: Crown of Static
Jukebox Musical x Post-Apocalyptic

Decades after the Great Silence fried every algorithm on Earth, music survives only in one battered, solar-powered jukebox bolted into a sand-buggy. Enter Prince Dorian Vexley III — deposed, sunburned, and clinging to a velvet sash that no longer means anything — who discovers his off-key power ballads are the only thing that can power the machine and rally the scattered survivors of Dust-Town. As a warlord called The Maestro hunts the jukebox for himself, Dorian must choose between reclaiming his lost throne and becoming the karaoke messiah the wasteland actually needs. It's a toe-tapping fight for the soul of humanity, and Dorian only knows the high notes.

Starring: Exiled Royal
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Jukebox Musical and Post-Apocalyptic coexist uneasily, our hero — a Exiled Royal — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sunburned exiled prince in tattered royal finery belts into a microphone atop a rusted jukebox-buggy in a vast post-apocalyptic desert, shot like a gritty blockbuster one-sheet.",

Skeletal ruins of a collapsed city skyline shimmer on the distant horizon through heat haze. Drifting sand, scattered burnt cars, and tangled power lines recede into deep cinematic distance.",

Sweet Nothings, Deadly Tentacles
Creature Feature x Romantic Comedy

Mid-level accountant Marvin Pflug just wanted to forward one incriminating email about NaviCorp's "totally safe" deep-sea drilling — instead he unleashed Gerald, a forty-foot bioluminescent kraken who is, unfortunately, a hopeless romantic. As Gerald devours the boardroom and woos the cleaning staff, Marvin must protect both his anonymous-tip hotline and his clumsy crush, HR rep Brenda, who keeps misreading every catastrophe as a grand gesture. Featuring meet-cutes in flooded stairwells, a tentacle bearing roses, and one very confused subpoena. Love means never having to say you're squid-food.

Starring: Corporate Whistleblower
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Creature Feature setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Romantic Comedy. Our reluctant hero: a Corporate Whistleblower.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a panicked office whistleblower clutching a manila folder in a flooding corporate atrium while a giant glowing kraken tentacle delicately offers him a bouquet of roses.",
  "background": "The flooded glass atrium of a sleek corporate skyscraper at night, knee-deep seawater rippling across marble floors and reflecting emergency lights. Shattered floor-to-ceiling windows reveal a storm-lashed harbor skyline glowing teal and amber in the distance. Bioluminescent mist drifts between toppled office chairs and floating paperwork, giving the cavernous lobby eerie depth.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-orange cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze, subtle film grain, glossy wet reflections",

The Encore Interrogation
Psychological Drama x Buddy Cop

Burnout glam-rocker Sid "The Velvet Reaper" Calloway hasn't played a sold-out arena since 1998, but he HAS unintentionally witnessed a murder backstage at a county fairground. Now he's handcuffed to no-nonsense Detective Marisol Vega for witness protection — and she refuses to let him relapse into either heroin OR the third verse of his only hit. As the case spirals, the film slows into unsettling psychological territory: who is the man behind the eyeliner, and which of his memories are real? It's a buddy-cop thriller having a full nervous breakdown in the back of an unmarked Crown Victoria.

Starring: Washed-Up Rock Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Psychological Drama show had a baby with a Buddy Cop miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Washed-Up Rock Star.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A washed-up rock star in smudged eyeliner sits handcuffed beside a stern detective in a rain-streaked car interior, lit by passing neon, in a moody photoreal one-sheet.",
  "background": "The dim interior of an unmarked sedan parked outside a closed-down concert venue at night, windshield streaked with rain. Beyond the glass, a blurred bokeh marquee flickers half-broken neon letters and wet asphalt reflects red and blue light. Deep shadow swallows the back seat, leaving the two figures isolated in pooled streetlight.",

Objection, Your Honor, It's a Metaphor
Satire x Legal Drama

Dezzy Pruitt, a perpetually broke performance artist who pays rent in "exposure," accidentally sues a billionaire art collector and somehow wins the right to represent herself in the highest-stakes copyright trial of the decade. Armed with zero legal training, one inspirational beret, and a tendency to answer cross-examination with interpretive dance, she discovers the courtroom is just the world's most expensive open-mic night. As the system closes in, Dezzy must prove that her "found garbage installation" is genuine art — or owe damages she'll be paying off in the afterlife. A blistering, deadpan satire about justice, gentrification, and the unbearable pretentiousness of being right.

Starring: Struggling Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Struggling Artist, alone, in the rain. The genre? Satire. The vibe? Unmistakably Legal Drama. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched young artist in a beret stands defiantly in a marble courtroom doorway clutching a paint-smeared briefcase, lit like a prestige legal thriller but absurdly out of place.",
  "background": "The towering interior of a grand neoclassical courtroom at dusk, polished marble columns and dark mahogany benches receding into shadow. Rain somehow falls indoors through a leaking skylight, catching shafts of cold light. An ornate gold scales-of-justice statue looms in the deep background, slightly tilted as if off-balance.",

The Hard Sell at Dead Mule Gulch
Survival x Acid Western

Mort Pennywhistle is the finest vacuum-and-encyclopedia salesman the dusty Territory of New Perdition has ever ignored — until a peyote-laced sasparilla sends his wagon, his mule, and his sample case tumbling into a sunbaked canyon with no road out. Now sixty miles from water, hallucinating a chorus line of singing scorpions, Mort must barter, charm, and door-to-door his way past cannibal claim-jumpers using only his briefcase of brushes and an unkillable sales pitch. Every man he meets wants him dead. Every man he meets is also a potential customer. Closing the deal has never been this literal.

Starring: Traveling Salesman
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Survival meets Acid Western... but the protagonist is a Traveling Salesman.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sunblasted, dust-caked traveling salesman in a ruined three-piece suit clutches a battered sample case as he staggers through a surreal blood-orange desert canyon, shot like a gritty photoreal Western survival epic.",
  "background": "A vast cracked-clay canyon at brutal high noon, jagged red rock walls rising into a sky bleached white-gold with heat shimmer. Bleached cattle bones and a half-buried, overturned peddler's wagon litter the foreground sand. Distant mesas warp in waves of mirage, and a lone circling vulture is a speck against the punishing sky.",

Dead Man's Bluff
Cold Case x Spaghetti Western

Forty years after a stagecoach massacre went unsolved, silver-tongued grifter "Honest" Cassidy Vane rolls into the sun-bleached town of Perdido with a rigged faro deck and a dead man's pocket watch he can't explain. When the town's last surviving witness recognizes the watch, Cassidy must solve the very cold case he's been conning everyone about — before a black-clad gunman with a ledger of grudges closes the file permanently. Part dusty whodunit, part squint-and-shoot showdown, it's a swindler's race to outdraw the truth. The con of his life, and it's also the truth.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Cold Case and Spaghetti Western coexist uneasily, our hero — a Con Artist — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered con artist in a dust-caked frock coat stands at high noon in a dead Old West main street, fanning a deck of cards while a tarnished pocket watch dangles from his fist, a black-clad gunman blurred in the deep background.",
  "background": "A wide, sun-scorched spaghetti-western main street of cracked clapboard storefronts and a leaning saloon, dust devils drifting across rutted dirt. Heat shimmer warps the horizon where distant mesas glow ochre under a bleached midday sky. Far down the street a lone black-coated figure stands small and ominous in the haze.",

How the West Was Beamed
Weird West x Alien Invasion

Dust-bowl drummer Cornelius "Corny" Pratt can sell ice to a snowman and a snake-oil tonic to a dying mule — which is why nobody believes him when he insists the new settlers in Bone Hollow are extraterrestrials wearing very bad human suits. Armed with a wagon of dubious miracle products and a smile that won't quit, Corny must out-talk a hive of body-snatching invaders before the whole frontier gets "harvested." His pitch of a lifetime now has the highest stakes imaginable: the planet. Closing the sale has never been this apocalyptic.

Starring: Traveling Salesman
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Weird West setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Alien Invasion. Our reluctant hero: a Traveling Salesman.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A dusty frontier salesman stands before his wagon at twilight as a colossal saucer hovers over a Western boomtown, beams of green light raking the main street.",
  "background": "A wide deserted Western main street at dusk, weathered timber storefronts and a saloon receding into haze. Above the town hangs an enormous metallic flying saucer half-obscured by dust and rolling cloud, casting vertical beams of sickly green light onto the dirt road. The far horizon glows orange-pink with a dying sun, scattered tumbleweeds frozen mid-roll.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-and-amber cinematic grade, photorealistic, heavy volumetric dust haze, lens flare on the saucer beams",
  "aesthetics": "epic, absurd, ominous, sun-baked, retro-sci-fi spectacle",
  "lighting": "warm low-angle sunset key on the salesman contrasted against cold green rim light spilling down from the saucer overhead.",

Badge of Honor: License to Filing
Farce x Spy Thriller

On his first day, rookie officer Dennis Pollard accidentally pockets a defecting scientist's microfilm, mistakes a deadly assassin for his carpool buddy, and triggers an international incident before lunch. Now every elegant villain in Geneva wants him dead — and they all think he's a legendary super-agent because he keeps tripping into ventilation shafts at the perfect moment. Armed with a parking-ticket booklet and zero clearance, Dennis must bluff his way through a tuxedo gala, a high-speed Vespa chase, and a code phrase he absolutely cannot remember. The fate of the world rests in the trembling hands of a man who's still on probation.

Starring: Rookie Cop
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Rookie Cop, alone, in the rain. The genre? Farce. The vibe? Unmistakably Spy Thriller. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched, terrified rookie cop in a too-big uniform stands under a single streetlight in the rain clutching a parking-ticket booklet like a weapon, framed exactly like a sleek spy-thriller one-sheet.",
  "background": "A rain-slicked European cobblestone alley at night, neon reflections smeared across wet stone, a distant ornate gala building glowing gold in deep soft focus. Heavy diagonal rain streaks catch the light, and a black town car idles ominously in the misty background. The depth falls off into moody bokeh and drifting fog.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-orange cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze, fine rain streaks lit from behind",
  "aesthetics": "sleek espionage tension undercut by visible panic, glossy noir, deadpan absurdity",

Objection, Your Adulthood
Coming-of-Age x Dark Comedy

Twenty-five-year-old Marcus Pry passed the bar on his first try and immediately accepted a case nobody else would touch: suing the universe for emotional damages. Armed with a thrifted suit two sizes too big and a misplaced faith in the goodness of all people, he discovers his idealistic crusade has accidentally exposed a small-town graft ring run by his own mentor. As witnesses keep "retiring to Boca" and his cactus becomes his only loyal client, Marcus must decide whether growing up means selling out — or finally winning the one case that matters. It's a bleakly hilarious courtroom fable about losing your innocence one objection at a time.

Starring: Idealistic Lawyer
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Coming-of-Age meets Dark Comedy... but the protagonist is a Idealistic Lawyer.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A baby-faced young lawyer in an oversized suit stands alone in a vast empty courtroom clutching an enormous briefcase, lit like a melancholy comedy.",
  "background": "The interior of a grand but slightly shabby municipal courtroom, deep wood paneling receding into shadow with rows of empty wooden benches. Dust motes drift in slanted afternoon light from tall arched windows. The judge's bench looms huge and unoccupied in the soft-focus background, an American-style flag drooping beside it.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, warm muted cinematic grade with slight desaturation, photorealistic, fine film grain, volumetric window light",

The Eldritch Hour: A Town That Couldn't Stop Singing
Cosmic Horror x Jukebox Musical

Burned-out arts reporter Marla Voss takes one last lousy assignment — covering why the dead-end town of Harrow Falls hasn't shut up about its annual sing-along. Turns out the locals can't stop performing classic radio hits because a tentacled god beneath the reservoir feeds on jazz hands and earnest key changes. Marla's the only one immune to the cosmic earworm, which makes her humanity's last cynical hope and a terrible duet partner. To save everyone, she must out-belt an unknowable deity using nothing but sarcasm and a karaoke machine. The encore will end the world.

Starring: Cynical Journalist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Cosmic Horror and Jukebox Musical coexist uneasily, our hero — a Cynical Journalist — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A cynical woman journalist clutches a vintage microphone in a flooded town square as a colossal tentacled silhouette rises behind a glowing marquee, terrified townsfolk frozen mid-song around her.",
  "background": "A decaying small-town main street at night, half-submerged in black reflective floodwater that mirrors a sickly green sky. A towering eldritch silhouette of writhing tentacles and a single luminous eye looms over the rooftops in the misty distance. Old theater marquees and storefront signs flicker, their warm bulbs reflecting in the water, while low fog curls between the buildings.",

High Noon Press: A Western in Overtime
Revisionist Western x Sports Drama

Sheriff Wade "Dusty" Cobb has kept the dirt-road town of Pinch, Arizona peaceful for nineteen quiet years — until a drifting bookie informs him that, per local statute and a forgotten 1887 charter, all disputes must now be settled by full-court basketball. With the railroad barons fielding a ringer squad and the saloon doubling as a locker room, Dusty must trade his six-shooter for a sweatband and coach a team of grizzled ranchers to the championship. He's got bad knees, a tragic backstory, and exactly one inspirational speech left in him. Loser leaves the territory. Forever.

Starring: Small-Town Sheriff
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Small-Town Sheriff discovers their quiet life is actually a Revisionist Western, they must master the genre conventions of Sports Drama to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered small-town sheriff stands mid-dusty-street in full Western gear but cradling a basketball, framed like a showdown at high noon outside a saloon-turned-gymnasium.",
  "background": "A sun-scorched frontier main street stretching to a vanishing point, false-front wooden buildings flanking a rutted dirt road. One saloon has a regulation basketball hoop bolted crookedly above its swinging doors, a sagging net swaying in the heat. Distant mesas shimmer under a blazing white midday sky with low dust haze hanging in the air.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, sun-baked golden-amber cinematic grade, fine film grain, photorealistic, volumetric dust haze, lens flare",

The Lighthouse Keeper Did It (Probably)
Cold Case x Screwball

For thirty-one years, Brunhilde "Bunny" Halvorsen has kept the Sorrowcliff Light burning and the same unsolved murder taped to her refrigerator. When a fast-talking insurance adjuster shipwrecks on her rocks demanding answers, her solemn cold-case obsession collides with his caffeinated optimism and a missing lobster diary. Bunny just wants to grieve into her microwave salisbury steak in peace; the universe wants a slamming-door farce with two suspects, one rowboat, and a foghorn that goes off at the worst possible moments. The truth is out there. So is dinner, and it's getting cold.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Cold Case in act one, Screwball by act three, and a Lighthouse Keeper sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered lighthouse keeper clutches a microwave dinner and a yellowed case file at the base of a fog-shrouded lighthouse while a frantic man in a soggy suit waves a notebook behind her, shot like a glossy theatrical one-sheet.",
  "background": "A craggy storm-lashed coastline at dusk dominated by a tall white lighthouse with a glowing lamp room piercing thick rolling sea fog. Jagged wet rocks and a half-beached wooden rowboat sit in the lower foreground, sea spray frozen mid-air. The horizon fades into bruised purple clouds with a faint moon, giving deep atmospheric layers.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, moody teal-and-amber cinematic grade, volumetric fog, photorealistic, fine film grain, natural skin texture",
  "aesthetics": "moody nautical noir, screwball comedic timing, melancholic absurdity, premium streaming one-sheet",
  "lighting": "the rotating lighthouse beam rakes warm amber light across the foreground figures against cold blue dusk, with soft rim light from the lamp room and practical glow from the microwave dinner.",
  "palette": [
    "#15303f",
    "#e8a24c",
    "#c9d6dd",
    "#7a2f3a",
    "#0a1418"
  ],

      "type": "text",
      "text": "IN THEATERS THIS FOG SEASON",
      "desc": "small spaced all-caps release line in amber above the billing block, centered",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.22,
      "y": 0.885,
      "w": 0.56,
      "h": 0.03
    }
  ]
}
Abracadabra: Rules of Engagement
Psychological Horror x Military Action

Reluctant Vegas illusionist "The Astonishing" Dale Crick thought his biggest threat was a heckler in the front row — until his sold-out residency dissolves into a black-ops nightmare where his own assistants are tactically flanking him with collapsible swords. As the theater's walls breathe and his rabbit starts giving hand signals, Dale can't tell if he's hallucinating or being hunted by a special-forces unit that only exists when the lights go out. Armed with nothing but flash paper, a deck of cards, and crippling stage fright, he must execute the greatest disappearing act of his life. The reviews are killer. Literally.

Starring: Stage Magician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Psychological Horror setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Military Action. Our reluctant hero: a Stage Magician.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A terrified tuxedoed stage magician crouches in a fog-drenched, blood-lit theater while shadowy combat-geared figures advance from the wings, his playing cards scattering through the smoke like shrapnel.",
  "background": "The interior of a derelict grand theater shrouded in low-lying smoke, red velvet curtains hanging in tatters under a single failing spotlight. Deep in the shadowed wings, the silhouettes of armed tactical operatives advance in formation, laser sights cutting thin lines through the haze. Rows of empty seats recede into oppressive blackness behind a fogged proscenium arch.",

The Good, The Bad, and The Defibrillator
Spaghetti Western x Medical Drama

Faded glam-rock legend "Dusty" Calloway rides into the parched border town of Sangre Seca chasing a comeback gig — only to find the town has no saloon, just an overworked frontier triage tent. When a noon duel ends with two slugs in the sheriff, Dusty discovers his only marketable skill since rehab is a steady hand under pressure. Now he's the reluctant chief surgeon of a one-horse hospital where every gunfighter codes at high noon and the only anesthetic is mezcal. He'll have to stop the bleeding, restart a heart, and maybe his career — all before the cartel's specialist rides in for a second opinion.

Starring: Washed-Up Rock Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Spaghetti Western setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Medical Drama. Our reluctant hero: a Washed-Up Rock Star.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered long-haired rock star in a dusty leather duster stands in a sun-scorched western street holding defibrillator paddles like dueling pistols, photographed as a gritty cinematic one-sheet.",
  "background": "A baking, deserted frontier main street at high noon, rutted dirt road vanishing toward distant adobe buildings and a crooked wooden infirmary tent flying a faded red-cross flag. Heat shimmer rises off the ground and a lone tumbleweed catches dry wind. The sky is a bleached, dust-hazed amber dome with one circling vulture.",

Last Dance in Dead Man's Holler
Cold Case x Jukebox Musical

Reclusive thriller novelist Maren Vance hasn't left her cabin — or finished a sentence out loud — in eleven years. But when a forty-year-old unsolved disappearance reopens, the entire town of Dead Man's Holler can only communicate through spontaneous, fully-choreographed renditions of jukebox classics. To crack the case, Maren must overcome her crippling stage fright and lip-sync her way through honky-tonk confessions, doo-wop alibis, and one devastating power-ballad red herring. The clock is ticking: if she doesn't name the killer by the last song, the bar — and the truth — close forever.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Cold Case and Jukebox Musical coexist uneasily, our hero — a Reclusive Author — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered female author stands frozen mid-step on a glowing jukebox-lit barroom floor as evidence photos and townsfolk caught mid-song swirl around her in a smoky, neon-drenched honky-tonk.",
  "background": "Interior of a derelict roadside honky-tonk bar at midnight, deep perspective receding toward a dusty stage. A vintage Wurlitzer jukebox glows amber and magenta in the back corner, light spilling across a scuffed wooden dance floor. Cigarette haze hangs in volumetric beams; faded missing-person flyers paper the back wall under a buzzing neon sign.",

Blade & Evidence: The Goblin in the Stone
Sword & Sorcery x Forensic Procedural

Fresh out of the Royal Constabulary Academy, Deputy Wren Halloway is the first cop in the Kingdom of Vorlach to dust a cursed broadsword for fingerprints. When a beloved warlord turns up petrified mid-sneeze and a goblin syndicate starts lawyering up, Wren must pair medieval magic with a forensics kit that fits in a satchel. Her partner is a 900-year-old skeleton who keeps contaminating the crime scene. Solve the murder, or the whole realm gets subpoenaed by demons.

Starring: Rookie Cop
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Sword & Sorcery show had a baby with a Forensic Procedural miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Rookie Cop.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rookie fantasy cop in studded leather armor crouches over a glowing magical crime scene, holding evidence tweezers up to a floating ember of arcane dust, lit like a prestige crime drama",
  "background": "A torch-lit stone hall of a ruined castle keep at night, with crumbling gothic arches receding into smoky darkness. Faint runic chalk lines and numbered yellow evidence markers glow on the flagstone floor, blending caution-tape ritual circles with crime-scene staging. Cold blue moonlight spills through a shattered stained-glass window while warm torch flames flicker in the depth of frame.",

The Penalty Box
Crime Drama x Locked Room

Disgraced ex-goalkeeper Marco "The Wall" Delvecchio — banned for life over a bribery scandal he swears he didn't take — is the only guest who stays behind when a luxury ski-lodge investor is found garroted in a chalet sealed by a blizzard. No signal, no exits, six suspects, and a detective who already hates athletes. Marco has until the snow clears to prove he didn't do it, using the only skills he has left: reading angles, anticipating the shot, and never, ever flinching. Turns out the perfect crime has one weakness — a man who's spent his whole life watching the net.

Starring: Disgraced Athlete
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Crime Drama meets Locked Room... but the protagonist is a Disgraced Athlete.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A brooding former goalkeeper stands in a locked, snowbound chalet great-room, lit by a single chandelier, evidence and suspects blurred behind him in a tense crime-drama one-sheet.",
  "background": "Interior of a vast alpine chalet great-room sealed against a howling blizzard, tall frosted windows packed solid with snow and no visible exit. Deep shadows pool in the timber rafters while a dying fire throws orange across an antique rug stained dark near an overturned chair. Faint silhouettes of five uneasy guests are scattered in the soft-focus background, each lit just enough to read suspicion.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, cold teal shadows with warm amber firelight, fine film grain, photorealistic, volumetric haze, cinematic contrast",

Stiff & Garnish: A Killing in the Kitchen
Buddy Cop x Food Doc

Mortician Cornelius Boggs has embalmed bodies for thirty silent years — until a celebrity chef drops dead face-first in his own foam, and the precinct partners Cornelius with hot-headed gastro-detective Rico Vance. Together they work the city's underground supper-club homicide ring, narrating each crime scene like a Michelin-starred food documentary, complete with reverent slow-motion plating and tearful interviews with the deceased's pastry chef. Cornelius brings a corpse's calm; Rico brings a deathly fear of cilantro. They have 48 hours and one reservation to crack the case before the killer's next "tasting menu" claims another diner.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Undertaker, alone, in the rain. The genre? Buddy Cop. The vibe? Unmistakably Food Doc. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{

Whodunit, Baby, One More Time
Amateur Sleuth x Jukebox Musical

Mild-mannered crossword editor Marvin Pesh wants nothing more than to finish his Sunday puzzle in peace. But when his neighbor turns up dead clutching a karaoke remote, an ancient prophecy declares Marvin the "Chosen Crooner" — the only sleuth who can solve crimes, but exclusively by belting reworked classic-rock hits at suspects. Now he must learn jazz hands, decode clues hidden in song lyrics, and out-harmonize a murderous a cappella cult before the third-act key change kills him. He really, really cannot carry a tune. Solving the case means singing for his life.

Starring: Reluctant Chosen One
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Reluctant Chosen One discovers their quiet life is actually a Amateur Sleuth, they must master the genre conventions of Jukebox Musical to survive.

The Unfinished Masterpiece
Cosmic Horror x Sword & Sorcery

Bartholomew Quill, a perpetually broke portrait painter behind on rent and self-respect, accepts a commission to paint the "true face" of a god that should not be looked at. Big mistake. As his canvas begins screaming and his brushes weep ink that whispers in a dead language, Bartholomew discovers the only way to seal the gate he accidentally opened is to FINISH the painting — while wielding an enchanted palette knife against tentacled critics from beyond the stars. Part starving-artist tragedy, part barbarian sword-and-sorcery epic, fully unhinged. Art is pain. So is this.

Starring: Struggling Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Struggling Artist, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cosmic Horror. The vibe? Unmistakably Sword & Sorcery. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-soaked struggling painter stands defiantly in a ruined stone temple, brandishing a glowing palette knife like a sword as a colossal tentacled cosmic entity erupts from his own canvas behind him.",
  "background": "A crumbling moss-eaten stone temple interior open to a violent storm, towering broken pillars receding into darkness. Behind the figure, an enormous easel holds a half-finished canvas from which a vast iridescent tentacled mass bursts forth, glowing eldritch green and bleeding into a churning star-pocked void in the sky above. Cold rain streaks through shafts of sickly light, mist pooling around shattered marble floors.",

Sutures of the Sundered Realm
Forbidden Romance x High Fantasy

Dr. Maren Vell quit medicine after a thousand-hour shift broke her, fleeing the city to drink mead in a dying frontier kingdom. But when the realm's exiled Frost Prince is carried in with an arrow lodged a finger from his heart, she's the only one who can save him — and falling for enemy royalty is punishable by the Crown's executioner. Now Maren must keep him alive, keep their affair secret, and stitch up a plague that's quietly devouring the village. Caffeine helps. Magic helps more. Her cynicism, somehow, helps most of all.

Starring: Burned-Out Surgeon
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Forbidden Romance and High Fantasy coexist uneasily, our hero — a Burned-Out Surgeon — must save... something. Probably a town.

The Keeper of Sorrows
Grimdark x Forbidden Romance

In a plague-rotted kingdom where loyalty is paid in blood, embittered lighthouse keeper Bram Oathmourn has one sworn duty: guide the dead king's war-fleet safely home so it can raze the rebel coast. Then a half-drowned rebel commander washes onto his rocks — devastatingly handsome, infuriatingly principled, and exactly the man Bram is sworn to doom. Now every flicker of the great lamp is a choice between the oath that defines him and the forbidden ache that's ruining him. Brooding, rain-soaked, and unbearably horny, it's a war epic where the deadliest weapon is eye contact.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Grimdark setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Forbidden Romance. Our reluctant hero: a Lighthouse Keeper.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A storm-lashed black stone lighthouse at night with two rain-soaked men locked in a charged near-embrace on the rocks below, lit by the dying amber beam above.",
  "background": "A jagged volcanic coastline under a churning ash-grey storm sky, waves smashing against black rocks and throwing sea-spray into the wind. A towering, weather-scarred medieval stone lighthouse rises center-frame, its great lamp casting a single weak amber beam through the rain. Distant on the horizon, the silhouettes of dark war-galleys with tattered black sails creep toward shore.",

Eureka! A Lovely Apocalypse
Screwball x Dystopian

In the rusted ration-state of New Cheerville, disgraced inventor Dr. Bibi Voltaire builds a machine to abolish all human emotion — and accidentally invents the world's first sarcastic toaster instead. Now wanted by the Ministry of Mood for "excessive zaniness," she's stuck sharing a one-room bunker with her insufferably chipper ex-husband, a defecting state censor, and a malfunctioning surveillance drone that keeps falling in love. As the regime tightens its grip, Bibi has 48 hours to either save the dystopia or get everyone killed by talking too fast. It's a screwball revolution with terrible timing and excellent banter.

Starring: Mad Scientist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Screwball show had a baby with a Dystopian miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Mad Scientist.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A frazzled female mad scientist in a singed lab coat strikes a frantic comic pose amid a grim totalitarian megacity, clutching a sparking sarcastic toaster while a heart-eyed surveillance drone hovers beside her.",
  "background": "A towering brutalist dystopian metropolis of concrete spires and giant flickering propaganda screens stretches into a smog-orange dusk. Searchlights sweep across rows of identical gray apartment blocks plastered with peeling 'STAY CALM' banners. Steam vents and tangled cables fill the cramped foreground alley where the action takes place, giving deep layered atmosphere.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, dystopian teal-and-rust cinematic grade, volumetric haze, photorealistic, fine film grain, crisp practical lighting",
  "aesthetics": "absurdist, frantic, retro-futuristic, grimy yet comedic, energetic",
  "lighting": "harsh sodium-orange streetlight from above mixed with cold blue screen glow and bright sparks from the toaster rim-lighting the scientist's wild hair.",

The Murder Ballad of Hollow Pine
Indie Folk Musical x Psychological Horror

Disgraced detective Cyrus Vane—stripped of his badge for "hearing confessions that weren't there"—drifts into Hollow Pine, a fog-drowned mountain town where every resident answers questions only by singing. As townsfolk vanish one by one, Cyrus realizes the killer leaves clues exclusively in the lyrics of a haunting folk ballad nobody admits writing. To solve it he must learn the song's final unsung verse—but anyone who completes the melody dies. Part toe-tapping hootenanny, part slow-dread nightmare, it's a whodunit where the truth harmonizes back.

Starring: Disgraced Detective
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Disgraced Detective stumbles into a Indie Folk Musical conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Psychological Horror.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A haunted, rain-soaked detective stands on a misty forest road clutching a battered banjo case as ghostly townsfolk hum from the treeline behind him.",
  "background": "A narrow gravel mountain road dissolving into dense pine forest choked with low ground fog at blue hour. Faint amber cabin windows glow deep in the trees, suggesting a watching town. The sky is a bruised charcoal grey, heavy and oppressive, with thin shafts of weak light cutting through the canopy.",
