Murder Ballad: A Toe-Tapping Investigation
True Crime x Screwball

Banjo-strumming folk troubadour Earl "Two-Capo" Dabney returns to tiny Possum Hollow to headline the Heritage Pickle Festival, only to discover the festival's prize-winning gourd has been STOLEN — and the local true-crime podcast is already framing him for it. To clear his name before the town goes bankrupt, Earl must out-sleuth a deadpan documentary crew, a fast-talking ex-fiancée who runs the sheriff's office, and his own incurable habit of narrating crimes in three-part harmony. Every clue becomes a verse. Every suspect gets a chorus. Possum Hollow has eleven hours and one tambourine to save itself.

Starring: Folk Musician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where True Crime and Screwball coexist uneasily, our hero — a Folk Musician — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A bewildered folk musician clutches a banjo on a small-town main street while documentary crew gear and crime-scene tape frame him, shot like a prestige true-crime poster with a screwball wink",
  "background": "A sleepy rural American main street at dusk, crooked storefronts and a hand-painted PICKLE FESTIVAL banner sagging overhead. Yellow crime-scene tape stretches across a fruit stand in the mid-ground where a single empty gourd pedestal sits spotlit. Soft fog rolls between telephone poles and a distant water tower, with warm porch lights flickering on as twilight deepens.",

Saucier: A Detective's Reduction
Magical Academia x Food Doc

Expelled from the Brackmoor Academy of Applied Sorcery after his evidence-summoning spell accidentally vaporized the dean, ex-Detective Inspector Roland Pim now hosts a streaming food documentary nobody asked for. Armed with a cracked scrying mirror and a microwave he insists is "artisanal," Roland tries to crack the cold case of who poisoned the realm's last great soufflé — while ugly-crying over a frozen shepherd's pie. Each episode he interviews enchanted ingredients that refuse to confess. It's a whodunit, a cookery show, and a breakdown, plated beautifully.

Starring: Disgraced Detective
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Magical Academia in act one, Food Doc by act three, and a Disgraced Detective sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rumpled trench-coated detective sits hunched over a glowing microwave in a candlelit magical academy kitchen, steam rising like clues, tears catching the light as a single floating enchanted soufflé hovers beside him.",
  "background": "A cavernous Gothic academy kitchen at night, stone arches and towering shelves of glass jars glowing with faint magical light. Copper pots hang from iron racks, soft beams of moonlight slice through tall leaded windows behind. Deep shadows recede into a haze of culinary steam and floating dust motes.",

The Last Confession of Sam Spadehands
Satire x Hardboiled Detective

Father Augustine "Gus" Malone hasn't believed in anything since the diocese replaced the choir with a synergy app. So when the parish bingo cash vanishes and a stained-glass saint turns up shattered, he hangs a trench coat over his cassock and starts working the case like the gumshoe God never called him to be. The trail of lies runs from the bishop's brunch to a megachurch with valet parking, and every suspect wants absolution before an alibi. It's a hardboiled whodunit where the only thing more corrupt than the killer is the parking-lot expansion committee.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Satire meets Hardboiled Detective... but the protagonist is a Disillusioned Priest.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary priest in a rumpled trench coat over a black cassock stands in a rain-slicked cathedral doorway lit by neon, holding a cigarette and a rosary like a detective's badge.",
  "background": "A narrow nighttime alley beside a gothic stone cathedral, wet cobblestones throwing back smeared neon reflections of a buzzing 'OPEN 24 HRS — CONFESSIONS' sign. Steam rises from a sewer grate and rain streaks diagonally through the air. Far down the alley a single stained-glass window glows sickly amber, half-shattered.",

The Made Man Who Never Was
Heist x Psychological Horror

Sal "Knuckles" Provolone has spent twenty years breaking kneecaps for the Carnevale family — until he notices his apartment has no doors, his wife answers to three different names, and the same pigeon keeps landing on the same ledge at 3:14 PM exactly. Sal slowly realizes his entire "life" is an elaborate long con staged inside his own skull to crack the location of a vault only his subconscious remembers. To survive the unraveling, the enforcer must out-think the architects haunting his memory — before the heist finishes itself and erases him for good. A claustrophobic descent where the only thing scarier than the crew robbing him is what they're robbing from.

Starring: Mob Enforcer
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Mob Enforcer discovers their quiet life is actually a Heist, they must master the genre conventions of Psychological Horror to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary middle-aged mob enforcer stands in a dim apartment hallway that loops impossibly into itself, his fractured reflection multiplying in warped mirrors, shot like a prestige psychological thriller one-sheet.",
  "background": "A narrow 1970s tenement hallway with peeling sepia wallpaper that recedes into an unnatural infinite loop, the far end bending back toward the viewer in a subtle non-Euclidean warp. Cold fluorescent light bleeds from a single flickering fixture, and faint duplicate doorframes ghost into shadow on either side. Deep haze pools at the floor, swallowing detail into blackness.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated cold cyan-and-tobacco grade, photorealistic, fine film grain, volumetric haze, subtle lens distortion at frame edges",
  "aesthetics": "claustrophobic, paranoid, uncanny, prestige psychological thriller, noir dread",
  "lighting": "a single flickering overhead fluorescent casts hard top-down light on the man while the looping hallway dissolves into cold shadow.",
  "palette": [
    "#0E1418",
    "#243038",
    "#6B5436",
    "#A9B6BC",
    "#C4452F"
  ],
  "bg_brightness": 28,

Till Death Do Us Part: The Eternal Embrace Exposé
Cult Exposé x Marriage of Convenience

Mortician Desmond Quill has buried half the county and married none of it — until a documentary crew catches him signing a hasty marriage license to inherit his late mentor's funeral home. The catch: his "bride," Posy, is the recruiter for the Eternal Embrace, a wellness commune that worships embalming fluid and matching beige jumpsuits. As the cameras roll, Desmond must fake honeymoon bliss while smuggling out evidence that the cult's "afterlife retreats" are suspiciously one-way. It's a slow-burn marriage of convenience wrapped in a true-crime takedown, and someone keeps rearranging the caskets.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Undertaker, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cult Exposé. The vibe? Unmistakably Marriage of Convenience. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal documentary-style poster of a rain-soaked undertaker standing beside a serene cult recruiter, both clutching a marriage certificate, with a hearse and beige-robed figures lurking behind glass.",
  "background": "A dim funeral-home interior at night seen through a rain-streaked front window, mahogany caskets and lily arrangements receding into shadow. Behind a fogged glass partition, a blurred row of beige-robed cult members stands in unnaturally calm formation. Cold blue exterior streetlight bleeds in from the left while a warm amber chandelier glows from the right, creating documentary tension.",

Crystal Ball Evidence: A Murder Foretold
Forensic Procedural x Rock Opera

Madame Velveteen Crow runs the only psychic-hotline-slash-crime-lab in the rust-belt town of Cinder Hollow, where she's legally required to sing every prophecy in full power-ballad. When the mayor turns up dead — and her tarot deck names the killer three days BEFORE the body cools — the skeptical state coroner demands chain-of-custody on a vision. Now Velveteen must reconcile lab results with séance results before a wailing electric-guitar prophecy comes true and the whole town goes up in pyrotechnic flame. It's spectral evidence versus spectrographs, scored to a 110-decibel organ solo.

Starring: Fortune Teller
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Forensic Procedural and Rock Opera coexist uneasily, our hero — a Fortune Teller — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:

Encore at the End of the World
Spy Thriller x Cult Exposé

Burnout glam-rocker Dex Mallory hasn't had a hit since 1994, so when a slick "wellness label" offers him a comeback tour, he signs without reading the fine print. Turns out the label is a deep-cover surveillance front — and somewhere between the laser-tag-themed safe house and a microwave lasagna, Dex realizes the tour is recruiting fans into a doomsday cult that worships HIM. Now armed with a wireless headset mic and the worst hangover in espionage history, Dex must out-spy his handlers and de-program his own fan club before the encore literally ends civilization. A thriller about reverb, redemption, and reading contracts.

Starring: Washed-Up Rock Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Spy Thriller in act one, Cult Exposé by act three, and a Washed-Up Rock Star sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:

  "high_level_description": "A haggard aging rock star stands center-stage in a swirl of stage smoke, clutching a microphone like a weapon while shadowy robed figures and surveillance equipment loom behind him, shot like a glossy theatrical one-sheet.",

Footnotes: The Musical Truth
Mockumentary x Dance Musical

Reclusive literary genius Bartholomew Quill hasn't left his crumbling Vermont farmhouse — or finished his second novel — in nineteen years. So a desperate documentary crew moves in to film "the writing process," only to discover that Bartholomew can no longer speak in prose: every confession, panic attack, and grocery list now erupts as a fully choreographed Broadway dance number. As the town of Maple Hollow faces bankruptcy without his long-promised bestseller, the camera rolls while Bartholomew tap-dances his way toward one terrifying final chapter. It's a tell-all about a man who can only tell it in jazz hands.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Mockumentary and Dance Musical coexist uneasily, our hero — a Reclusive Author — must save... something. Probably a town.

The Last Verse at Loon Lake
Disaster x Cozy Whodunit

When folk darling Marigold Pike returns to the sleepy lakeside hamlet of Loon's Rest for a charity hootenanny, the only thing duller than her ex's banjo solo is the weather report — until the dam upriver starts groaning. Then the festival's beloved fiddler turns up drowned in three inches of suspiciously rising water. With the valley flooding by the hour and twelve sweater-clad suspects trapped in a tilting lodge, Marigold must solve the murder before the whole town goes under — all while resisting the smoldering park ranger who keeps shouting evacuation orders directly into her soul. Cozy, doomed, and unbearably romantic.

Starring: Folk Musician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Disaster x Cozy Whodunit. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Folk Musician.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A windswept folk musician clutches her guitar on a tilting lakeside dock as a towering wall of floodwater surges behind her, lit by a stormy dusk.",
  "background": "A picturesque mountain lake village at dusk, quaint timber lodge and string lights along a wooden dock in the mid-ground. A monstrous wall of churning brown floodwater rises against the far treeline, swallowing the valley. Low storm clouds, drifting mist, and golden lamplight reflecting on the rising water create deep cinematic perspective.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-and-amber cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze, fine film grain, dramatic wide-angle",
  "aesthetics": "cozy dread, romantic doom, rustic Americana, brooding storm tension",

      "w": 0.4,

    }
  ]

The Gentleman Swindler's Awakening
Coming-of-Age x Period Romance

Seventeen-year-old Percival "Pip" Hargrove is the finest forger of love-letters in all of Brighton, fleecing lovesick aristocrats one counterfeit sonnet at a time. But when he's hired to ghostwrite a courtship for the dazzling, sharp-tongued Lady Arabella — who immediately sees through every con — Pip discovers the one swindle he can't run is on his own heart. As his lies snowball into a season-long social catastrophe, he must choose between the perfect grift and his first, terrifyingly genuine feeling. A bittersweet drawing-room comedy about growing up, falling hard, and finally telling the truth.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Coming-of-Age setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Period Romance. Our reluctant hero: a Con Artist.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal Regency-era one-sheet of a roguish teenage con artist clutching a fan of love-letters while a skeptical young noblewoman eyes him across a candlelit ballroom.",
  "background": "A grand candlelit Georgian ballroom at dusk, marble columns and tall arched windows receding into soft golden gloom. Chandeliers glitter with warm bokeh in the deep background while a blurred crowd of period dancers swirls in muted silks. Faint coastal moonlight spills through the far windows, hinting at the Brighton seafront beyond.",

The Bachelor Always Rings Twice
Reality Competition x Second-Chance Romance

Reformed grifter Sully Marchetti talks his way onto a primetime dating show using a fake billionaire backstory — only to discover that the franchise's new producer is Delphine, the woman he conned and abandoned at an airport seven years ago. Now she controls every rose ceremony, every confessional cut, and every chance he has to win back the one mark he never wanted to leave. As the cameras roll, Sully has to run the longest con of his life: telling the truth. Equal parts elimination chaos and aching second-chance romance, it's the most honest lie ever broadcast.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Con Artist, alone, in the rain. The genre? Reality Competition. The vibe? Unmistakably Second-Chance Romance. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-soaked man in a soaked tuxedo clutches a single wilting rose under harsh TV stage lights while a woman in a producer's headset watches him from a glowing control-room window above.",
  "background": "A glossy reality-dating-show set at night, a curved staircase and string-light backdrop blurred behind sheets of falling rain. Above and to the right, a bright control-room window glows with banks of monitors. Wet stage flooring reflects pink and blue light, deep cinematic depth fading into haze.",

Commander-in-Grief: The Casserole Conspiracy
Military Action x Amateur Sleuth

Senator Roland Pike spent twelve years building the perfect campaign — until a botched black-ops raid he secretly authorized goes catastrophically sideways, killing his only loyal aide. Now hunted, disgraced, and weeping into a 99-cent Salisbury steak every single night, Roland reinvents himself as an amateur detective to clear his name and unmask the shadow donor who set him up. Armed with a corkboard, a laser pointer, and zero forensic training, he stumbles from gunfights to garden-party interrogations. A whisper-thriller about ambition, grief, and the precise reheating time of frozen lasagna.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Military Action in act one, Amateur Sleuth by act three, and a Ambitious Politician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sweaty, tearful politician in a torn suit clutches a glowing microwave dinner like a sacred relic in a war-torn campaign office, lit like a high-stakes military thriller.",
  "background": "A ransacked campaign headquarters at night, American flags shredded and smoldering, overturned podiums and scattered classified folders across the floor. Through a shattered window, a city skyline glows under helicopter searchlights and rising smoke. Deep shadows recede into a cluttered war-room of corkboards strung with red yarn.",

Blade & Blaster: The Two-Bit Heist of Dagmar Vex
Space Opera x Sword & Sorcery

Dagmar Vex is a small-time crook who fences cursed amulets out of a cargo hold and has never once paid for parking his hover-mule. When he accidentally swipes a sword that's also a sentient plasma rifle — and it won't stop narrating his crimes — every wizard-bounty-hunter in the Nine Star-Holds wants his head. Now Dagmar has eleven hours to return the blade, dodge a dragon that files taxes, and save the only frontier town that still lets him run a tab. He is spectacularly unqualified. The sword agrees.

Starring: Small-Time Crook
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Space Opera and Sword & Sorcery coexist uneasily, our hero — a Small-Time Crook — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A grimy roguish thief clutches a glowing sword-rifle hybrid on a dusty alien frontier street as twin moons and a distant starship loom, lit like a gritty space-western epic",

Far down the avenue a battered cargo freighter idles in heat-haze beneath two large pale moons and a smear of nebula. Volumetric dust drifts through low orange backlight; depth falls off into hazy distance.",

The Rapture Index
Disaster x Conspiracy Thriller

Father Elias Crane lost his faith somewhere between the third funeral and his second bottle of communion wine. So when a basement archive in the Vatican reveals that an extinction-level solar flare was predicted — and deliberately concealed — by a shadow conclave, he's the only man unholy enough to chase the truth. With twelve hours until the sky catches fire, Elias races a smoldering cabal, a seismologist who tempts him toward the very vows he abandoned, and his own creeping certainty that maybe the world deserves it. Salvation is classified. Damnation is right on schedule.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Disaster x Conspiracy Thriller. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Disillusioned Priest.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sweat-drenched priest in a torn cassock stands in a cracked cathedral nave as a blood-orange firestorm sky splits open behind him, conspiracy documents fluttering through the air.",
  "background": "A vast Gothic cathedral interior whose vaulted ceiling has cracked open to reveal a churning apocalyptic sky burning blood-orange and ash-gray. Stone pillars lean and fracture, dust and embers drift through god-rays of harsh light. In the deep background a city skyline glows with distant fires beyond the shattered stained glass.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-and-ember cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze, fine film grain, practical firelight",
  "aesthetics": "apocalyptic dread, sacred decay, smoldering tension, conspiratorial paranoia, sweaty intimacy",

Whodunnit, Reverb?
Surreal Comedy x Amateur Sleuth

Once the frontman of stadium gods Velvet Hernia, Dax Marrow now lives in a leaking lake house where the furniture occasionally floats and his goldfish gives advice. When his last surviving bandmate is found drowned in a hot tub full of his own platinum records, the police rule it "a vibe" — so Dax, armed with three working brain cells and a karaoke machine, appoints himself detective. Between sobbing into lukewarm microwave lasagna and interrogating a suspiciously articulate roadie, he discovers the truth was hidden in a B-side all along. The reverb knows everything.

Starring: Washed-Up Rock Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Surreal Comedy in act one, Amateur Sleuth by act three, and a Washed-Up Rock Star sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{

Hosanna in the Highest Conspiracy
Conspiracy Doc x Tragic Musical

Father Lucian Voss lost his faith, found a corkboard, and never looked back. In this "documentary," the chain-smoking, dangerously handsome priest croons torch-song confessionals about the Vatican's secret thermostat cabal while a trembling investigative cellist follows him through candlelit catacombs. Every revelation is sung. Every duet ends in tears or sin. As Lucian inches toward the truth — and toward his cameraman, Brother Diego — the choir gets louder, the lighting gets steamier, and somebody's clearly going to die before the third act key change.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Conspiracy Doc x Tragic Musical. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Disillusioned Priest.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sweat-sheened disillusioned priest mid-anguished-song in a candlelit catacomb plastered with conspiracy strings, shot like a moody true-crime doc that wandered into an operatic melodrama.",
  "background": "A vaulted stone catacomb crammed with a red-string conspiracy wall: hundreds of pinned photographs, newspaper clippings, and tangled crimson thread converging on a single golden crucifix. Hundreds of dripping votive candles recede into smoky darkness, throwing flickering shadows up the damp masonry. Faint stained-glass light bleeds from a far archway, hazy with incense.",

The Honorable Affair
Prestige Drama x Period Romance

Newly elected Senator Cassius Thorne discovers his predecessor was murdered to bury a treasonous ledger — and the only witnesses are the powdered aristocrats of a 1814 estate frozen in time by their own scandal-bound etiquette. To extract the truth, Cassius must abandon backroom horse-trading and master the lethal arts of the calling card, the chaperoned waltz, and the devastatingly worded letter. Every confession requires a courtship; every alliance demands he risk his heart to the icy Lady Ravensmere, who may be both his killer and his soulmate. In a world where a misplaced glove ends careers, the most dangerous campaign is romance.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Ambitious Politician stumbles into a Prestige Drama conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Period Romance.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A modern-suited politician and a Regency-gowned noblewoman stand back-to-back in a candlelit grand ballroom, divided by eras yet bound by intrigue, on a moody photoreal one-sheet.",
  "background": "An opulent 1810s ballroom interior with gilded columns, towering arched windows, and a vast crystal chandelier dripping warm light. Deep perspective recedes into shadowed colonnades where masked guests linger as soft silhouettes. Drifting candle-smoke haze and dust motes hang in the air, lending depth and a faint sense of secrets.",

The Chosen Three: Eliminated With Extreme Prejudice
Buddy Comedy x Reality Competition

Dale Prunch, a mild-mannered parking enforcement officer, is informed by an ancient prophecy (and a very enthusiastic man named Gary) that he alone can stop a citywide conspiracy. The catch: the cult's secrets are sealed by reality-TV logic, so Dale must survive themed elimination challenges, confessional-booth betrayals, and a "synergy round" with his accidental best friend, a disgraced former game-show host. Together they bicker, form an alliance, and lie to a confessional camera nobody can find. Saving the world means outlasting the other contestants — and surviving the dramatic reveal music.

Starring: Reluctant Chosen One
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Reluctant Chosen One stumbles into a Buddy Comedy conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Reality Competition.

  "high_level_description": "Two mismatched men stand back-to-back under blazing reality-show stage lights inside a crumbling ancient temple repurposed as a competition arena, immunity-idol medallions glowing between them.",

The Quiet Game: A Tournament of Hands
Sports Doc x Surreal Comedy

Vincent "The Whisper" Kovac retired from contract killing to dominate the world's most prestigious competitive thumb-wrestling circuit — and a reverent documentary crew has captured every dread-soaked, inexplicably sweaty moment. As rival champions vanish under suspicious circumstances, talking-head interviews dissolve into fever dreams of slow-motion knuckle-cracking and a referee who may be Death itself in a polo shirt. Vincent insists he's a changed man, yet his thumb still knows 14 lethal pressure points. It's the feel-good sports story of the year, if your feelings are mostly unease and longing.

Starring: Retired Assassin
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Sports Doc x Surreal Comedy. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Retired Assassin.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal sports-documentary one-sheet of a brooding retired assassin gripping a rival's hand mid thumb-wrestle under a single dramatic spotlight, shot like a prestige doc still.",
  "background": "A cavernous, near-empty tournament arena bathed in deep shadow, with a distant blurred crowd lit only by scattered phone lights like embers. A lone overhead spotlight carves a column of haze down to a regulation thumb-wrestling table draped in black felt. The depth falls off into oppressive darkness, lending the scene a sacred, funereal hush.",

Hostile Tides: A First Contact Romance
First Contact x Enemies-to-Lovers

Captain Eli "Barnacle" Rourke has hated exactly two things his whole life: paperwork and anything smarter than him. So when a luminous squid-shaped alien named Vessel-7 crash-lands its cathedral-sized ship in his fishing lanes and demands he stop "polluting the broadcast frequency with bait smell," it's loathing at first sight. But as the two trade insults across a translation buoy and accidentally save each other from a kraken-class warship, Rourke realizes the most terrifying first contact isn't with the cosmos — it's with feelings. Humanity's fate now hinges on whether one stubborn old sailor can apologize.

Starring: Grizzled Sea Captain
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a First Contact setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Enemies-to-Lovers. Our reluctant hero: a Grizzled Sea Captain.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered sea captain stands on the deck of a trawler glaring up at a glowing translucent alien being descending over a stormy ocean, the two locked in a charged hostile standoff under bioluminescent light.",
  "background": "A churning slate-grey ocean at dusk under heavy storm clouds, with a colossal smooth alien craft hovering low and casting teal light across the whitecaps. The horizon glows faint orange where the sun has nearly set. Mist and sea-spray drift through the frame giving deep atmospheric layering.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-orange cinematic grade, photorealistic, volumetric sea haze, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "epic, romantic-tension, brooding, awe-struck, salt-soaked",
  "lighting": "Cold bioluminescent teal glow from the alien being rakes across the captain's face while a warm lantern from the boat lights him from below, creating a clash of warm and cool light.",
  "palette": [
    "#0E2A33",
    "#1F6E78",
    "#46E0D2",
    "#D98A3A",
    "#0A1418"
  ],
  "bg_brightness": 38,
  "elements": [
    {
      "type": "obj",
      "text": "",

Landslide: A Comedy of Tumbleweeds
Screwball x Neo-Western

State Senator Dale Brisco has a five-year plan, a fake folksy accent, and a campaign bus that just exploded outside a town called Consequence. Stranded in the dusty middle of nowhere with a runaway intern, a stolen prize bull, and a rival candidate who keeps showing up in increasingly improbable disguises, Dale's slick fast-talking screwball charm slowly erodes into squinting, sweaty Neo-Western reckoning. To win the swing district he never asked to be stuck in, he must outdraw a sheriff, woo a diner waitress with the only honest soul in the county, and stop crying into gas-station lasagna. Democracy has never been this hot, this dumb, or this dehydrated.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Screwball in act one, Neo-Western by act three, and a Ambitious Politician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A sweat-soaked politician in a wrinkled suit stands in a cracked desert main street at golden hour, clutching a microwave dinner and a campaign sign, a prize bull behind him, framed like a Western showdown that's gone hilariously wrong.",
  "background": "A sun-blasted neo-Western small-town main street with peeling clapboard storefronts, a faded DINER sign, and a leaning campaign billboard, stretching toward distant mesas and a hazy dust-blown horizon. Heat shimmer warps the air above cracked asphalt where a single tumbleweed rolls past an overturned, lightly smoking campaign bus. Deep perspective draws the eye down the empty street into burnt-orange dusk.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, warm dusty golden-hour grade, fine film grain, volumetric haze, photorealistic, cinematic wide-angle",

Madame Pustula's Inevitable Forecast
Farce x Body Horror

Madame Pustula, a small-town fortune teller with a tragic addiction to slamming doors and hiding lovers in armoires, foresees one tiny detail in every client's future: the exact hour their flesh begins to rearrange itself. When a misdelivered prophecy causes the mayor, the vicar, and three identical insurance salesmen to start sprouting extra elbows during a single chaotic dinner party, Pustula must keep the bursting boils a polite secret. Doors slam, organs migrate, and nobody acknowledges the screaming. A farce so well-timed it's practically gangrenous.

Starring: Fortune Teller
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Fortune Teller, alone, in the rain. The genre? Farce. The vibe? Unmistakably Body Horror. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched fortune teller stands under a flickering parlor sign in the rain, clutching a crystal ball while a tasteful, glistening tendril of extra fingers curls from inside her sleeve, shot like a prestige horror-comedy one-sheet.",
  "background": "A narrow cobblestone European side-street at night during heavy rain, a crooked storefront fortune-telling parlor with a buzzing neon palm sign behind her. Deep wet reflections pool on the stones, fogged windows glow amber, and a row of distant doorways recedes into misty darkness suggesting farce-like exits. Sheets of rain catch the light in long streaks.",

The Ballad of Real Things
Psychological Drama x Surreal Comedy

Wendell Crane, a soft-spoken folk musician with a banjo and a recurring dream about his own knees, slowly realizes his entire life has been a brooding prestige drama — long silences, meaningful staring, a wife who keeps "discovering things about herself." To escape the crushing weight of all that meaning, Wendell must learn the rules of Surreal Comedy: befriending a sentient harmonica, accepting that his porch is now in France, and performing a breakup song to a jury of identical men named Gary. If he can't make the universe laugh, the third act will make him cry. He has chosen the harmonica.

Starring: Folk Musician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Folk Musician discovers their quiet life is actually a Psychological Drama, they must master the genre conventions of Surreal Comedy to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary folk musician stands on a wooden porch that floats in an endless wheat field while a giant harmonica hovers like a moon and a jury of identical suited men sits in the grass below, all shot like a prestige drama poster.",

The Vanishing Act of Silas Crow
Acid Western x Psychological Drama

Silas Crow, a third-rate vaudeville magician with a flask of mescaline and a guilty conscience, gets stranded in the cursed mining town of Hollow Mercy, where the sun never quite sets and everyone he's ever wronged seems to be living. When the local sheriff is sawn in half — for real — Silas realizes the only way out is to confront the buried trauma he keeps hidden up his sleeve. Part hallucinatory gunslinger fever dream, part slow-burn therapy session at high noon, this is a Western where the deadliest weapon is honesty. Pick a card. Any card. They're all the same card. It's you.

Starring: Stage Magician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Stage Magician stumbles into a Acid Western conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Psychological Drama.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weather-beaten stage magician in a dusty tailcoat stands alone on a cracked desert flat at an eerie purple dusk, fanning playing cards that dissolve into crows against a bruised acid-Western sky.",
  "background": "An endless cracked alkali desert under a surreal, oversaturated dusk where the sky bleeds magenta into sickly amber, a low sun smeared and doubled on the horizon like a hallucination. A skeletal ghost-town water tower and a few collapsed timber buildings sit far in the deep distance, warped by heat shimmer. Dust hangs in suspended, motionless clouds, lending an unnatural dreamlike stillness.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, heavy heat-haze distortion, oversaturated psychedelic magenta-amber grade with crushed shadows, photorealistic, fine film grain, volumetric dust",

The Comeback Skin
Dark Comedy x Body Horror

Washed-up action legend Rance Delacroix hasn't headlined a hit since the '90s, so when a boutique "wellness studio" offers a miracle anti-aging treatment, he says yes before reading the waiver. The cream works terrifyingly well — his face de-wrinkles, his abs return, and his old skin starts peeling off into a fully sentient, scheming younger clone who wants his career, his agent, and his Malibu deck. Now Rance must out-deadpan a conspiracy of beautiful body-horror doppelgängers while literally holding himself together. A grisly, deadpan comedy about vanity, sequels, and never reading the fine print.

Starring: Aging Movie Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Aging Movie Star stumbles into a Dark Comedy conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Body Horror.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a grizzled aging movie star whose flawless younger face is peeling away from his real weathered face like a wet mask, lit like a glamorous but sinister awards portrait.",
  "background": "A dim, opulent Hollywood dressing room with a wall of vanity mirror bulbs glowing in soft bokeh behind the figure. Deep shadow pools in the corners while peeling, translucent sheets of human skin drift in the air like discarded film negatives. A faint red EXIT-style glow bleeds in from screen right, giving the velvet wallpaper a bruised tone.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, fine film grain, glossy skin texture, desaturated amber-and-bruise grade, photorealistic, volumetric haze",
  "aesthetics": "darkly glamorous, grotesque, deadpan, uncanny, prestige-horror",
  "lighting": "warm vanity-bulb key light raking across the face from the left, cold blue rim light separating the peeling clone, soft red fill in the shadows.",
  "palette": [

    "#caa15e",
    "#7c2a2a",
    "#d8c4a0",
    "#2b3a44"

    },
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "THE COMEBACK SKIN",

The Mating Season of Agent Vesper
Nature Doc x Forbidden Romance

Retired superspy Vesper Cole has traded dead drops for a cliffside puffin sanctuary, narrating the secret lives of seabirds for a wildlife streaming network. Her cover is going perfectly — until the BBC-style documentary crew sends a velvet-voiced narrator, Lars, who turns out to be the enemy handler she once left at the altar. Now, as a hidden cartel hunts them both across the breeding colony, Vesper must protect her flock, decode a microfilm sewn into a nesting gull, and resist the one man her old agency forbade her to love. Forbidden romance has never been so feathered.

Starring: Ex-Spy
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Nature Doc show had a baby with a Forbidden Romance miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Ex-Spy.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A windswept sea-cliff at golden hour where a poised woman in a trench coat and a tuxedoed man lock eyes across a colony of nesting puffins, photographed like a sweeping prestige drama one-sheet.",
  "background": "A dramatic North Atlantic sea cliff at golden hour, jagged emerald-and-ochre rock face plunging to a churning teal ocean far below. Thousands of seabirds wheel through misty sea spray in the deep background, with a distant lighthouse silhouetted on a far headland. Low sun rakes across the cliff, throwing long shadows and igniting the haze.",

Hard Sell: Maximum Commission
Car-Chase Thriller x Workplace Comedy

Dale Pruitt sells industrial-grade laminating machines, and he is very good at it — which is precisely why a cartel of corporate spies wants his demo model. After a 90-mph freeway chase ends with his Buick wedged into a regional sales office, Dale realizes the safest place to hide is undercover... as the new guy in Cubicle 14B. Now he must dodge assassins, survive trust-fall exercises, and close the biggest deal of his life before Karen from Accounting figures out his badge is laminated wrong. He cries into a microwave Salisbury steak between every act. It's never been more dangerous to exceed quota.

Starring: Traveling Salesman
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Car-Chase Thriller in act one, Workplace Comedy by act three, and a Traveling Salesman sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A frazzled middle-aged salesman in a rumpled tie crouches behind an open car door in an office parking garage, briefcase clutched to his chest, as a chase car skids in flaming background blur.",

Deep behind the hero a sedan fishtails in a smear of motion blur, headlights streaking and tire smoke billowing toward a glass-walled cubicle office visible at the far end. Volumetric haze and oil-slick reflections pool on the concrete floor, fading to murky teal shadow at the edges.",

You Light Up My Apocalypse
Romantic Comedy x Cosmic Horror

Eddie Marsh keeps the loneliest lighthouse on the coast, where his only companions are a temperamental foghorn and the unspeakable tentacled god slowly waking beneath the harbor. When chipper marine biologist Dawn rows ashore to study "unusual barnacles," Eddie discovers two terrifying truths: the Old One demands a human bride by the next full moon, and he has catastrophic, knee-buckling feelings for the woman it picked. Now he must woo Dawn, sabotage an eldritch wedding, and keep an entire town from being lovingly devoured. Turns out cosmic dread and first-date jitters feel exactly the same.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Romantic Comedy and Cosmic Horror coexist uneasily, our hero — a Lighthouse Keeper — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a nervous lighthouse keeper and a smiling woman standing on rocky cliffs while a colossal tentacled silhouette rises from a moonlit sea behind the lighthouse beam.",
  "background": "A storm-bruised coastline at dusk, jagged wet rocks in the foreground giving way to a churning luminous sea. A weathered white lighthouse stands mid-frame, its golden beam slicing the fog. Behind it, half-hidden in low clouds, the impossibly vast silhouette of a tentacled leviathan looms against a sickly green-tinged moon.",

Code Black: A Rock Opera of the Outer Dark
Cosmic Horror x Rock Opera

Dr. Marguerite "Mags" Vane hasn't slept in nine days, hasn't blinked in three, and hasn't stopped singing since the thing on Table 4 woke up and harmonized back. When a patient flatlines into a non-Euclidean god, the only surgeon left in St. Abyssinia's Memorial must operate on a wound the size of the universe — using nothing but a scalpel, a power ballad, and a defibrillator that screams in a dead language. Each chest compression cracks reality wider. The chorus of the damned just wants her to hit the high note. It's a tragedy in three acts, four octaves, and infinite dread.

Starring: Burned-Out Surgeon
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Burned-Out Surgeon, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cosmic Horror. The vibe? Unmistakably Rock Opera. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A drenched, exhausted surgeon stands mid-belt in a flooded operating theater while a vast tentacled cosmic eye blooms above the surgical lights, lit like a live arena rock spectacle.",
  "background": "A cavernous, derelict hospital operating theater half-flooded with black water, surgical lights swinging on cables overhead. Behind and above, the ceiling has torn open into a swirling nebula of violet and sickly green, a colossal alien eye and writhing tentacles emerging from the cosmic rupture. Smoke and rain fall through dramatic shafts of stage-style light, deep volumetric haze receding into impossible darkness.",

Disruptor: The Unicorn Sings Alone
Psychological Drama x Jukebox Musical

Tech billionaire Caspian Vale built an empire on a smart-mirror that tells you you're enough — and he no longer believes it. As his board stages a coup and his neural-implant startup leaks his own intrusive thoughts to investors, Caspian's grip on reality unravels into spontaneous, devastating renditions of 1980s power ballads sung to an empty boardroom. Every panic attack arrives as a key change; every betrayal cues the synths. A haunting, hilarious descent in which the only honest voice left is the karaoke machine.

Starring: Tech Billionaire
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Psychological Drama meets Jukebox Musical... but the protagonist is a Tech Billionaire.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A lone tech billionaire stands isolated in a vast glass boardroom at night, gripping a wireless microphone like a lifeline under a single spotlight, his reflection multiplied in the dark windows.",
  "background": "A floor-to-ceiling glass corporate boardroom on the 80th floor at night, a glittering city skyline blurred far below through rain-streaked windows. Deep shadows pool in the corners while cold reflections of the man repeat across the dark glass panels. The long minimalist conference table stretches into shallow-focus emptiness behind him.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, cool desaturated teal grade with a warm spotlight pool, photorealistic, fine film grain, volumetric haze, cinematic",
  "aesthetics": "isolated, melancholic, glossy corporate, theatrical, quietly unhinged",
  "lighting": "a single hard overhead spotlight isolates the man in warm light while the rest of the room falls into cold blue darkness.",

      "text": "MERIDIAN HOUSE PICTURES PRESENTS  A DECLAN ROSSMORE FILM  JOAQUIN HALE \"DISRUPTOR: THE UNICORN SINGS ALONE\"  ELODIE FRANCK  MARCUS DEVANE  CASTING BY P. OKORO  MUSIC SUPERVISION BY LENA VOSS  DIRECTED BY DECLAN ROSSMORE",
      "desc": "Tiny condensed all-caps credits billing block in pale gray, centered, very small at the very bottom",
      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.12,
      "y": 0.94,
      "w": 0.76,
      "h": 0.04
    }
  ]
}
The Ballad of Wendell Frost
Psychological Drama x True Crime

Wendell Frost was the most beloved fingerpicking folk troubadour in the Blue Ridge — until three rival banjoists vanished after sharing his campfire. A grief-rattled documentary crew follows Wendell as he records a haunting concept album about each disappearance, never noticing his lyrics describe details police never released. Part confessional ballad, part slow-tightening interrogation, the film watches a gentle man unravel one verse at a time. By the final track, you'll wonder whether he's mourning the missing — or auditioning their epitaphs.

Starring: Folk Musician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Psychological Drama meets True Crime... but the protagonist is a Folk Musician.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered folk musician sits alone with a guitar in a dim cabin while documentary lights and an evidence-board loom behind him, shot like a prestige true-crime drama.",
  "background": "A cramped wood-paneled mountain cabin interior at night, lit by a single practical lamp. On the back wall hangs a string-and-photo evidence board with three faded portraits connected by red thread and pushpins. Soft volumetric haze drifts through the air; the depth falls off into shadow at the edges.",

The Last Draft at Dead Mule Gulch
Spaghetti Western x Coming-of-Age

Reclusive novelist Cormac "Quill" Bellweather has spent eleven years in a sun-blasted desert shack, refusing to finish his masterpiece and shooting the doorknob off anyone who mentions deadlines. Then thirteen-year-old runaway Pearl shows up demanding to be his editor — and his apprentice gunslinger. As a vengeful publisher and his posse of bounty-hunting literary critics ride into town for the overdue manuscript, the grumpy hermit and the brave kid must finish the book, draw fast, and grow up together. One of them is forty years too old for a coming-of-age story. It does not go smoothly.

Starring: Reclusive Author
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Spaghetti Western show had a baby with a Coming-of-Age miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Reclusive Author.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered reclusive author and a determined young girl stand back-to-back in a blazing desert ghost town, he gripping a fountain pen like a pistol, she clutching a stack of manuscript pages, in classic spaghetti-western standoff framing.",
  "background": "A cracked, sun-bleached frontier ghost town at high noon, with leaning wooden saloon fronts and a rusted water tower receding into heat shimmer. Dust devils curl across a wide dirt main street under a vast bone-white sky. Deep depth of field with distant mesas dissolving in ochre haze.",

Officer Dingle and the Reasonable Mountain
Surreal Comedy x High Fantasy

On his first day patrolling the kingdom-precinct of Glomthwaite, Rookie Constable Bartholomew Dingle is handed a cursed parking ticket, a sentient bicycle, and one impossible case: a mountain has filed a noise complaint against the entire town. As prophecy collides with paperwork, Dingle must arrest a sky that keeps apologizing, interrogate a duck who insists he's the rightful heir, and stop a wizard-developer from rezoning reality. He has no backup, no idea what's happening, and a soup that won't stop screaming in evidence. Saving the town would be easier if the town agreed it existed.

Starring: Rookie Cop
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Surreal Comedy and High Fantasy coexist uneasily, our hero — a Rookie Cop — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:

  "high_level_description": "A bewildered rookie cop in fantasy-tinged uniform stands at attention before an enormous floating cobblestone mountain that has a tiny medieval town stuck to its underside, photographed like a prestige fantasy one-sheet.",

The Last Warlock of Burbank
First Contact x Sword & Sorcery

Three-time Best Actor nominee Roland Castavere, 71, hasn't had a hit since the Reagan administration and now does commercials for arthritis cream. So when literal interstellar beings land on his Malibu deck and announce that the universe's first ambassador must be its "greatest performer of heroic sorcery," Roland — who once played a wizard in a forgotten 1987 flop — bluffs his way into the role. Armed with a prop staff, a SAG card, and zero actual magic, he must broker peace with an alien empire that mistakes his line readings for divine incantations. Method acting has never had higher stakes.

Starring: Aging Movie Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's First Contact meets Sword & Sorcery... but the protagonist is a Aging Movie Star.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered aging movie star in tattered ceremonial robes raises a glowing prop staff toward a colossal alien craft hovering over a fantasy-lit Los Angeles coastline, shot like a prestige sci-fi epic.",
  "background": "A dusk Malibu coastline where reality bends into otherworldly fantasy: jagged enchanted rock spires rise from the Pacific while a vast geometric alien mothership hovers low, casting beams through low-rolling sea fog. The distant LA hills glitter with both city lights and floating runic glyphs. Deep atmospheric haze creates layered depth from foreground cliff to far horizon.",

The Salesman of Forgotten Wares
Period Piece x Mythic Retelling

1888. Bartholomew Crick peddles thimbles, tonics, and pocket Bibles across the rain-drowned moors of northern England — until a flooded crossroads reveals that his sample case has been quietly selling pieces of forgotten gods. Now the old powers want their relics back, and they're willing to trade prophecy for a working pair of bootlaces. Cursed to never close a sale he doesn't regret, Bartholomew must out-haggle the Fates themselves before the last myth in his ledger comes due. A muddy, thunderstruck fable about commerce, faith, and a man who simply will not stop knocking.

Starring: Traveling Salesman
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Traveling Salesman, alone, in the rain. The genre? Period Piece. The vibe? Unmistakably Mythic Retelling. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A lone Victorian traveling salesman in a soaked greatcoat stands at a flooded crossroads clutching a glowing sample case, dwarfed by storm clouds shaped like ancient faces.",
  "background": "A desolate northern English moor at dusk, drowned in torrential rain, with a muddy crossroads and a leaning wooden signpost. Towering bruise-colored storm clouds churn overhead, their edges subtly forming colossal watching faces. Distant rolling hills fade into silver mist and sheets of falling water.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, desaturated period drama grade with cold steel-blue shadows and a single warm amber accent, photorealistic, heavy volumetric rain and haze, fine film grain",

Filibuster: Full Throttle for the Holidays
Car-Chase Thriller x Holiday Romance

Three weeks before a razor-thin Senate election, ruthless rising star Senator Margot Vance survives an armored-motorcade ambush and floors it into the snowbound town of Tinsel Hollow, where her only refuge is a cozy bed-and-breakfast run by a maddeningly cheerful widower named Caleb. Now she's doing 110 down icing roads while learning to bake gingerbread, dodging a black SUV that won't quit, and falling for the one man who has no idea who she is. Margot has always believed nothing matters more than winning — until a snowball fight, a stolen kiss, and a 200-mph candy-cane semi-truck pursuit make her reconsider her entire platform. Vote your heart. Buckle up.

Starring: Ambitious Politician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Car-Chase Thriller show had a baby with a Holiday Romance miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Ambitious Politician.

Cold Cases of the Third Kind
Alien Invasion x Cold Case

Mortician Edna Krell has buried this town's secrets for thirty years — so when alien dropships flatten the city, she treats the invasion like just another unsolved file gone cold. Armed with a corkboard, red yarn, and an embalmer's patience, Edna investigates each saucer attack as a "case," interviewing terrified survivors with the same flat monotone she uses on the bereaved. Her quirk: she insists every extraterrestrial deserves a proper death certificate. As the body count climbs and the ships keep landing, Edna realizes the only way to close the case is to interrogate the things that started it.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Alien Invasion setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Cold Case. Our reluctant hero: a Undertaker.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A stern elderly undertaker in a black funeral coat stands in a debris-strewn street holding a red-yarn evidence board while a colossal alien saucer hovers in the smoke-choked sky above her",

A massive metallic alien saucer hangs low in the haze above the rooftops, casting a cold beam down through volumetric smoke. Distant emergency lights flicker red and blue against a bruised purple sky.",

The Long Con at Coyote Flats
Neo-Western x Heist

Silver-tongued grifter Dallas "Three-Card" McRae rolls into the dying oil town of Coyote Flats planning to fleece one sucker and leave by sundown. Instead he uncovers a buried conspiracy involving a corrupt sheriff, a vanished water fortune, and a vault under the old saloon that nobody's cracked in forty years. The only way out alive is to assemble the worst crew in the county and pull the most elaborate heist the desert has ever seen — using nothing but lies, leverage, and a borrowed horse named Liability. Trouble is, every mark in town is running their own con right back at him.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Con Artist stumbles into a Neo-Western conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Heist.

Abracadaver: A Trick Gone Medieval
Time Travel x Sword & Sorcery

Las Vegas illusionist Reginald "The Astonishing Reg" Plonk botches his signature vanishing-box trick and accidentally yeets himself fourteen centuries into a kingdom that thinks his collapsible top hat is a holy relic. With nothing but a deck of marked cards, a chained dove, and crippling stage anxiety, Reg must defeat a genuine sorcerer who can do everything Reg only pretends to. Between battles he weeps quietly over microwave lasagna he smuggled through the time rift. To go home, he'll have to make the one thing disappear he's never managed before: an entire army.

Starring: Stage Magician
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Time Travel in act one, Sword & Sorcery by act three, and a Stage Magician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.

Layers of volumetric fog roll across muddy ground littered with broken spears. The deep background fades into stormy clouds lit by distant lightning.",

Knife to Meet You
Slasher x Romantic Comedy

Cynical true-crime journalist Margo Pruitt arrives in the perky lakeside town of Cuddle Hollow to debunk a masked killer she's certain is a publicity stunt — only to keep getting interrupted by Theo, an aggressively wholesome local florist who won't stop trying to set her up with someone. As bodies pile up between meet-cutes, Margo discovers the killer is keeping a "couples scrapbook" and has personally selected her as a romantic lead. Now she has eighteen hours, one cursed dating app, and a man who brings soup to a crime scene. Falling in love has never been so fatal.

Starring: Cynical Journalist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Slasher show had a baby with a Romantic Comedy miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Cynical Journalist.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a leather-jacketed female journalist holding up a recorder while a blood-spattered bouquet of roses dangles a hidden butcher knife behind her, a masked figure lurking in soft-focus.",
  "background": "A picturesque small-town lakeside main street at dusk, string lights glowing over storefronts and a heart-shaped balloon arch in the distance. The romantic warmth curdles into dread as fog rolls off the lake and one streetlamp flickers blood-red. Shallow depth keeps the cozy storefronts dreamy and slightly menacing behind the figures.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-and-rose cinematic grade, photorealistic, fine film grain, volumetric haze, subtle lens flare",

The Last Tango of Dr. Crescendo
Noir x Dance Musical

In rain-slicked Velvet City, where every confession comes with a kickline, disgraced biochemist Dr. Augustin Crescendo has invented a serum that makes the guilty literally dance until they sing the truth. When a torch singer turns up murdered mid-pirouette, the only witness is a saxophone, and Crescendo — twitchy, tuxedo-clad, allergic to his own success — must waltz through the underworld before the mob choreographs his final number. Trouble is, the serum's wearing off, and he can't stop tap-dancing. One wrong step and the whole town goes quiet forever.

Starring: Mad Scientist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Set in a world where Noir and Dance Musical coexist uneasily, our hero — a Mad Scientist — must save... something. Probably a town.
Image prompt:

  "high_level_description": "A photoreal noir one-sheet of a wild-haired scientist in a soaked tuxedo caught mid-dance-move under a single streetlamp on a rain-drenched cobblestone alley, glamorous danger swirling around him.",
  "background": "A narrow rain-slicked cobblestone alley in a 1940s metropolis at midnight, brick walls glistening, neon dance-hall signage bleeding red and teal reflections into puddles. Deep wet perspective recedes toward a foggy vanishing point where a lone vintage marquee glows. Volumetric haze and falling rain catch the light in long diagonal streaks.",

Six Feet Under Pressure
Rock Opera x Workplace Comedy

Dwayne "Digs" Pelletier is the most passionate junior undertaker at Everlasting Rest Funeral Home & Crematory, where the embalming fluid flows and so do the power ballads. When corporate slashes the budget and threatens to merge his beloved parlor with a discount casket outlet, Dwayne fights back the only way he knows how: a full-throated, lighter-waving rock opera performed during a Tuesday wake. With a synth-shredding hearse driver, a tone-deaf grief counselor, and a CEO who can only communicate in guitar solos, Dwayne must save everyone's jobs before the final encore — without waking the dearly departed.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Rock Opera show had a baby with a Workplace Comedy miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Undertaker.
Image prompt:

Concert-grade stage lighting cuts through thick low-lying fog, with a glowing polished casket on a bier in the mid-ground. Stained-glass windows backlit in electric magenta give the room a cathedral-meets-arena depth.",

The Cold Case Cantata
Tragic Musical x Cold Case

Idealistic public defender Esther Voss reopens a 30-year-old disappearance the whole city would rather forget — and discovers the only witnesses left will only speak in showtunes. Every clue arrives as a heartbreaking eleven o'clock number; every alibi is a duet that ends in tears. As Esther sings her way through dusty evidence lockers and grief-stricken jazz hands, she learns the case was buried by someone who hits a high C. Justice has never been this devastating, or this perfectly choreographed. Some songs were meant to stay unsolved.

Starring: Idealistic Lawyer
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Tragic Musical setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Cold Case. Our reluctant hero: a Idealistic Lawyer.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weary idealistic lawyer stands spotlit in a rain-soaked evidence archive, clutching a cold-case folder while a single theatrical spotlight pierces the gloom like a stage cue.",
  "background": "A vast, dim municipal evidence archive at night, endless steel shelving stacked with banker's boxes receding into shadow. A tall arched window leaks cold blue moonlight and faint rain streaks, while a lone warm theatrical spotlight cuts down from above as if the stage manager forgot to strike the set. Dust and haze hang in the air, giving the depth a melancholy, abandoned-theater feeling.",

The Disruptor: A Found Gold Rush
Weird West x Found Footage

When self-made trillionaire Cassius Vane livestreams the launch of his "frictionless frontier town" — a blockchain-backed boomtown in the high desert — the feed never stops recording, even after everyone disappears. Recovered from a buried server rack, the footage shows Cassius, alone, soaked, narrating to a drone that refuses to power down. Something old lives under the saloon, and it does not accept equity. As the dust storms close in and his investors stop blinking, Cassius keeps filming, certain the engagement metrics are worth his soul. They are not.

Starring: Tech Billionaire
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Tech Billionaire, alone, in the rain. The genre? Weird West. The vibe? Unmistakably Found Footage. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-soaked tech billionaire in a torn designer blazer films himself on a hovering drone in the middle of a ghostly Old West main street at dusk, the whole image framed like recovered found-footage.",
  "background": "A deserted Old West frontier main street stretching into deep perspective, weathered wooden saloons and a half-built 'smart town' with cracked solar panels bolted to the facades. A violent dust-and-rain storm rolls in from the horizon under a bruised purple sky, distant lightning silhouetting empty hitching posts. Mud, puddles, and overturned server crates litter the foreground.",
  "art_style": "shot on a low-light digital camcorder, found-footage aesthetic, slight lens distortion and chromatic aberration, handheld motion blur, desaturated cold grade with sickly green shadows, photorealistic, volumetric rain haze",

Cold Case, Colder Dinner
Spy Thriller x Dystopian

Disgraced detective Roy Petrov was the best spy the agency ever burned. After a botched extraction frames him for treason, Roy goes underground — only to surface forty years later in a chrome-plated megastate that has outlawed nostalgia, paper case files, and forks. Now the last man who remembers the truth must crack a conspiracy that spans three regimes, two identities, and one tragically reheated salisbury steak. He has the skills. He has the grudge. He just wishes the microwave would stop judging him.

Starring: Disgraced Detective
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Spy Thriller in act one, Dystopian by act three, and a Disgraced Detective sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A trench-coated detective sits alone at a chrome kitchen table in a dystopian high-rise, lit by the glow of a microwave, while a surveillance city looms through the window behind him.",
  "background": "A cramped brutalist apartment fused with a glass-walled megacity skyline at night, endless illuminated towers and floating surveillance drones receding into smog. Deep cold haze fills the room, broken by a single warm orange shaft of light from an open microwave. Rain streaks the towering window, neon billboards reflected in the puddled floor.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, teal-orange cinematic grade, volumetric haze, photorealistic, fine film grain",
  "aesthetics": "noir, dystopian, melancholy, surveillance-state cold, lonely",
  "lighting": "key light from a glowing microwave casts a warm amber glow on the detective's exhausted face while cold blue city light rims everything else.",

Till Death or Sundown
Marriage of Convenience x Neo-Western

Father Calloway Reyes lost his faith somewhere between the collection plate and the foreclosure notices, and now the only thing that can save his crumbling desert parish is a ranch deed tied up in a dead rancher's will — a will that pays out only to a married man. Enter Wren Marlowe, a tequila-smuggling widow who needs a husband's signature to keep her cattle off the bank's auction block. Their loveless courthouse vows come with one rule: no feelings, no questions, separate beds. But when a cartel of land-grabbing financiers and a very patient sheriff start circling, the fake spouses discover that pretending to love someone in a town this lawless is the most dangerous con of all.

Starring: Disillusioned Priest
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Picture a Marriage of Convenience setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Neo-Western. Our reluctant hero: a Disillusioned Priest.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered priest in a dusty cassock and a steely widow stand back-to-back at a desert crossroads at golden hour, wedding rings glinting, a dry frontier town behind them.",
  "background": "A sun-scorched neo-western landscape: cracked red-clay flats stretching toward distant mesas, a small dying town of clapboard storefronts and a leaning white chapel on the horizon. Open Plan, Open Floor: A Love Story
Workplace Romance x Disaster

Marcus Ojo is a sleep-deprived single dad and the only structural engineer who flagged that the new 90-story HarmonyCorp HQ was, technically, "a tube of crackers in an earthquake." Now it's his first day, the building is swaying like a yoga instructor, and his frosty boss Dana from Acquisitions is trapped in the same collapsing elevator — clutching his toddler's missing dinosaur. As floors pancake and the sprinklers turn the break room into a lazy river, Marcus and Dana must rappel down a dying skyscraper using HDMI cables and unresolved tension. Love means never having to say "I told you about the load-bearing wall." Snacks not included.

Starring: Single Parent
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Imagine if a Workplace Romance show had a baby with a Disaster miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Single Parent.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A handsome exhausted single dad in a soaked dress shirt shields a stern beautiful woman in business attire as a glass skyscraper buckles and crumbles in flames behind them, a tiny toy dinosaur clutched between their hands.",
  "background": "A towering corporate glass skyscraper splitting and collapsing mid-disaster, panes of glass exploding outward and office furniture tumbling through smoke. Deep vertical perspective looking up the cracking facade with orange fireballs erupting from upper floors. Dusk sky choked with grey debris haze and helicopter searchlights cutting through.",

The Eviction Notice From Nowhere
Found Footage x Portal Fantasy

Hard-boiled PI Sal Marchetti only films stakeouts on a busted camcorder because he doesn't trust the cloud. When a missing-persons case leads him to a damp Brooklyn basement, the camcorder catches something his eyes can't: a doorway that only opens for people who walk in backwards while reciting their own birthday. On the other side is Vorndahl, a kingdom of singing toll-trolls and a prophecy that names Sal "The Tracker of Three Worlds." To crack the conspiracy and save a dozen vanished tenants, Sal must obey portal-fantasy logic — exact words, exact riddles, no take-backs — all while his battery sits at 4%.

Starring: Private Investigator
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Private Investigator stumbles into a Found Footage conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Portal Fantasy.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A trench-coated private eye holds a glowing camcorder toward a doorway of swirling otherworldly light in a grimy basement, shot like a gritty horror-fantasy thriller one-sheet.",
  "background": "A decaying Brooklyn basement with sweating concrete walls, exposed pipes, and a single bare bulb. Behind the detective a battered wooden door hangs open, leaking a vortex of cold blue-green light that reveals impossible silhouettes of crooked towers and floating staircases in the distance. Dust and damp haze hang thick in the air, lit from the unnatural glow.",

The Salt and the Smoking Gun
Spy Thriller x Hardboiled Detective

Captain Esau Brine has run the same foggy ferry route for thirty years, talking mostly to his thermos and a one-eyed gull named Detective. Then a passenger drops dead clutching a microfilm sardine tin, and Esau learns his "quiet" harbor is a nest of international agents who've been using his boat as a dead-drop since 1987. Wanted by spies who don't believe a man can be this oblivious, Esau must trade his fishing knife for trench-coat monologues and learn to narrate his own life in gravelly past tense. Salt water runs in his veins; now so does the cold sweat of espionage.

Starring: Grizzled Sea Captain
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Grizzled Sea Captain discovers their quiet life is actually a Spy Thriller, they must master the genre conventions of Hardboiled Detective to survive.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered old sea captain in an oilskin coat stands on a fog-wrapped dock at dusk clutching a sardine tin, a silenced pistol tucked in his belt, while a sleek spy silhouette looms in the harbor mist behind him.",
  "background": "A misty industrial harbor at blue hour with looming shipping cranes and a rusted ferry hull receding into thick fog. Deep atmospheric perspective with wet cobblestone dock in the foreground and dim sodium lamps bleeding orange halos through the haze. A distant figure in a long coat stands half-dissolved in the mist near a gangway.",

Badge of Lies: The Rookie's Requiem
Rock Opera x Conspiracy Doc

Officer Danny Cruz wanted to write parking tickets and go home — instead he stumbles onto a citywide cover-up that he can only explain through power ballads. As shadowy councilmen redact evidence and his own precinct lip-syncs the official story, Danny belts out the truth in soaring choruses nobody asked for. Half talking-head exposé, half stadium rock spectacle, the film follows one tone-deaf-but-fearless rookie as he subpoenas the deep state one key change at a time. The conspiracy goes all the way to the top. So does his vocal range.

Starring: Rookie Cop
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Rock Opera meets Conspiracy Doc... but the protagonist is a Rookie Cop.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a young rookie cop standing in a rain-slicked interrogation room, mid-belt with arms thrown wide, while a string-board of red conspiracy yarn glows behind him like a stage backdrop.",
  "background": "A dim municipal interrogation room with a wall covered in a corkboard of pinned documents and crisscrossing red string, photographs redacted with black bars. Volumetric haze hangs in a single hard spotlight beam from above, and a foggy city skyline is barely visible through a barred window in the deep background. Wet concrete floor reflects scattered colored stage lights.",

The Tidekeeper of Tau Ceti
Hard Sci-Fi x Acid Western

On a tide-locked ocean planet, disgraced beam-tender Solomon Vane keeps the only photon lighthouse warning starships off the reefs of a black hole's gravity well. Then the light starts whispering in his dead wife's voice, the tides begin running backwards, and a sun-blistered drifter named the Cartographer rides in on a six-legged horse claiming the ocean is a single sleeping organism that wants Solomon to stop blinking. As relativity buckles and his own reflections start arriving early, Solomon must choose: keep the light, or finally let the dark have its dance. Dread-soaked, sweat-slicked, and dangerously horny.

Starring: Lighthouse Keeper
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Hard Sci-Fi x Acid Western. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Lighthouse Keeper.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weather-beaten lighthouse keeper stands at the base of a towering bio-mechanical photon lighthouse on an alien tidal shore, a blood-orange black-hole sun warping the sky behind him, shot like a sun-scorched cosmic western.",
  "background": "A vast tide-locked alien beach of black glassy sand stretching to a horizon where the ocean is frozen mid-recession into impossible standing waves. Above it a swollen distorted sun smears into an accretion ring around a black hole, bending the sky into a lensed arc. Heat shimmer and salt mist hang in the lower air; faint starships glint as specks near the warped horizon.",
  "art_style": "shot on 65mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, photorealistic, sun-bleached high-contrast grade with desaturated shadows and burnt-orange highlights, fine film grain, volumetric haze, lens flare from the warped sun",
  "aesthetics": "cosmic dread, sweaty frontier loneliness, sublime and erotic unease, sun-blasted desolation, hard-sci-fi grit",
  "lighting": "harsh low-angle blood-orange sunlight from a lensed black-hole sun rakes across the keeper and the lighthouse, throwing long distorted shadows over the black sand.",

Liquidation Event: A Galactic Comedy
Space Opera x Dark Comedy

When self-made trillionaire Dex Vantablack rockets off-world to dodge a planetary tax audit, he accidentally crashes his prototype mega-yacht into the capital throne-room of the Andromeda Dominion. Now the galaxy's worst dictators want him executed, his AI life-coach won't stop optimizing his "personal brand" during firefights, and the only ship willing to extract him is crewed by the disgruntled interns he laid off. To survive, Dex must do the one thing he's never done: apologize. Stakes are cosmic, ego is fatal, and the severance package is the universe itself.

Starring: Tech Billionaire
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
It's Space Opera meets Dark Comedy... but the protagonist is a Tech Billionaire.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A smug billionaire in a torn designer spacesuit stands triumphantly on a cracked alien throne dais while a vast burning space armada looms behind him, shot like a glossy big-budget sci-fi one-sheet.",

The Gilded Flock: An 1888 Exposé
Period Piece x Cult Exposé

Posing as a visionary "Prophet of the Electric Dawn," silver-tongued grifter Cornelius Vane has built a thriving commune of corseted true believers in foggy 1888 Yorkshire — all to fleece them of their inheritances. But a relentless lantern-jawed pamphlet-journalist is reconstructing his crimes interview by interview, and Vane's own followers are starting to take the gospel he invented terrifyingly seriously. Shot like a prestige period drama but framed like a modern docuseries — sepia talking-heads, ominous re-enactments, narration that knows too much. The con was perfect. The cult was an accident.

Starring: Con Artist
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Cold open: a Con Artist, alone, in the rain. The genre? Period Piece. The vibe? Unmistakably Cult Exposé. Cue title card.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A rain-soaked Victorian con man in a velvet frock coat stands alone on cobblestones beneath gaslight, photographed like a prestige period film but framed with documentary-style title cards.",
  "background": "A narrow 1888 English village street at night, slick wet cobblestones reflecting amber gaslamps, fog rolling between soot-stained brick facades and a distant chapel spire. Shallow depth fades the background into rainy haze, with faint silhouettes of bonneted figures watching from doorways. A single hanging lantern glows above the protagonist.",

Probationary: A Spell-Check Romance
Enemies-to-Lovers x Magical Academia

Rookie cop Dani Vasquez expected a quiet traffic beat — not a citywide enchantment crisis that gets her involuntarily enrolled at the Thornwick Academy of Applied Sorcery for "remedial citizen safety." Her instructor is Magister Cassian Vael, an insufferable, smug wand-snob who writes her up for improper hex containment on day one. They loathe each other through midterms, study-group standoffs, and one cursed library lockdown — right up until they don't. Between failing Potions and saving the academy from a sentient parking violation, Dani learns the hardest spell of all is admitting your nemesis makes excellent enemies-to-lovers material. She still cries into a microwave lasagna nightly. Tuition is murder.

Starring: Rookie Cop
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Enemies-to-Lovers in act one, Magical Academia by act three, and a Rookie Cop sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal one-sheet of a young uniformed cop and a brooding robed sorcerer standing back-to-back amid floating glowing spellbooks in a grand gothic academy hall, sparks crackling between their reluctant glances.",
  "background": "Interior of a colossal gothic university great hall at dusk, towering stained-glass windows leaking blue and amber light, soaring stone arches receding into haze. Floating candles and open books drift in midair, trailing faint ember-like magic. Long shadows stretch across a polished marble floor checkered with arcane inlays.",

The Quick and the Dead Inside
Romantic Comedy x Revisionist Western

Bounty hunter Cassidy "Tumbleweed" Boon rides into the dust-choked town of Mercy Falls to collect on her last big payday — only to discover her target is a heartbroken pie shop owner caught in a town-wide matchmaking scheme run by the mayor's lonely heart club. As wedding-bell romance threatens to derail frontier justice, Cassidy learns the only way to untangle the conspiracy is by the old code: high noon, hard truths, and a kiss she absolutely did not budget for. She's never missed a bounty. She's also never been on a second date. In Mercy Falls, the deadliest standoff is feelings.

Starring: Bounty Hunter
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Bounty Hunter stumbles into a Romantic Comedy conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Revisionist Western.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered female bounty hunter stands in a dusty western main street holding both a revolver and a wilting bouquet, framed for a photoreal romantic-comedy-meets-revisionist-western one-sheet.",
  "background": "A sun-bleached frontier town main street at golden hour, with crooked wooden storefronts, a faded 'MERCY FALLS GENERAL STORE' sign, and hanging paper wedding bunting strung between buildings. Dust hangs in the warm air and a distant church steeple catches the last light. Shallow depth keeps the storefronts soft while the foreground stays crisp.",
