Deck the Halls, Book the Crooks
Family Saga x Holiday Romance

Sheriff Maren Holloway has policed Tinsel Falls for twenty years on instinct, gut, and grudges — but when the town's beloved Pruett family clan starts unraveling over a forged will, a missing maple-syrup fortune, and three generations of Christmas Eve secrets, she discovers the only way to crack the conspiracy is to follow the sacred rules of the Hallmark playbook. To get a confession she must attend the tree lighting, fake-date her oldest rival, win the gingerbread bake-off, and accidentally fall for the suspect's brooding nephew. Justice has never smelled this much like cinnamon. She has until midnight on the 24th — and her badge — to close the case before the snow buries the truth.

Starring: Small-Town Sheriff
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
A Small-Town Sheriff stumbles into a Family Saga conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Holiday Romance.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weathered small-town sheriff in a snow-dusted uniform stands center-frame on a twinkling Main Street decked for Christmas, badge in one hand and a candy cane in the other, caught between a crime scene and a romance.",
  "background": "A picture-perfect snowy small-town Main Street at dusk, lined with brick storefronts strung in warm Christmas lights and evergreen garlands, a glowing town tree in the soft-focus distance. Fat snowflakes drift through the frame and a thin trail of yellow police tape flutters between two lamp posts. Cozy shop windows glow gold against the deep blue winter twilight, giving long receding depth down the street.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm anamorphic, shallow depth of field, warm-amber and icy-blue cinematic grade, volumetric snow haze, crisp festival bokeh, photorealistic",
  "aesthetics": "cozy, mischievous, festive, mock-noir warmth, holiday-movie charm",
  "lighting": "warm string-light key on the sheriff's face with cool blue twilight rim light and soft glints off falling snow",

  ],

      "palette": [],
      "x": 0.3,

Last Rites: The Eliminations
Reality Competition x Gothic Horror

Barnaby Crane has embalmed the dead in the same fog-drowned village for thirty quiet years, until a camera crew informs him he's been on a hit reality show called "Last Rites" the entire time — and the audience is voting. Now every coffin he closes triggers a "Sudden Death" round, the manor's portraits whisper his weekly rankings, and a spectral host with no eyes promises immortality to the final survivor. To stay in the game, Barnaby must master the rules of Gothic horror he never knew he was living: never investigate the crying, always bring a candle, and absolutely do not look behind the vanity. Confessional booth required.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Logline: When a Undertaker discovers their quiet life is actually a Reality Competition, they must master the genre conventions of Gothic Horror to survive.
Image prompt:

Deep in the background, two broadcast television cameras on tripods glow with red record lights, half-hidden by drifting mist. Last Rites Athletic Club
Sports Drama x High Fantasy

Mort Pallbright, a soft-spoken small-town undertaker, moonlights coaching the worst rec-league soccer team in the county — until a midnight burial cracks open a portal beneath the cemetery and his eleven benchwarmers are conscripted into the War of the Ember Throne. Now Mort must teach orcs the offside rule, embalm a fallen elf-king before the third act, and somehow win the regional championship in a realm where the losing team is sacrificed to a dragon. He copes the only way he knows: weeping quietly into a microwave Salisbury steak between drills. It's the underdog story of the millennium, and the casualties are real.

Starring: Undertaker
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Three-act structure: Sports Drama in act one, High Fantasy by act three, and a Undertaker sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
Image prompt:

Cracked earth and tilted gravestones bridge the two worlds at midfield, with a glowing rune-lit chasm splitting the grass. Last Saloon at the End of My Voice
Weird West x Indie Folk Musical

Burnout frontman Dez Calloway, whose stadium band imploded the same night the sun did, wanders into Hollowing — a dust-choked frontier town where every resident communicates only by singing aching, harmonized folk laments. To earn passage out, Dez must out-perform the Saloon, a sentient bordello-tavern that feeds on unfinished songs and lonely men. The catch: the more honestly he croons, the more the town wants to keep him forever, body and ballad. It's dread, it's longing, and somehow it's deeply, uncomfortably horny.

Starring: Washed-Up Rock Star
Behind the scenes
Seed pitch:
Pitch: Weird West x Indie Folk Musical. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Washed-Up Rock Star.
Image prompt:
{
  "high_level_description": "A weather-beaten rock star stands in a haunted desert main street clutching an acoustic guitar that glows from within, photographed like a moody A24 western.",
  "background": "A deserted Old West main street at dusk, warped wooden saloon facades leaning inward as if listening, lit by a swollen blood-orange sun half-sunk on the horizon. Fine dust and pollen drift through long shafts of light, and faint glowing sheet-music pages spiral up into the bruised sky. Deep perspective draws the eye down the empty rutted road toward the saloon doors.",
